question_text: If A and B are square matrices of the same order, then (A + B) (A – B) is equal to

options.explanation: (A + B) (A – B) = A (A – B) + B (A – B) = A 2 – AB + BA – B 2

explanation: (A + B) (A – B) = A (A – B) + B (A – B) = A 2 – AB + BA – B 2

question_html: If a matrix A is both symmetric and skew-symmetric, then A is a :
question_text: If a matrix A is both symmetric and skew-symmetric, then A is a :

options.explanation: If a matrix A is both symmetric and skew-symmetric, A’ = A & A’ = -A Comparing both the equations, A = -A A + A = 0 2A = 0 A = 0 then A is a zero matrix.

explanation: If a matrix A is both symmetric and skew-symmetric, A’ = A & A’ = -A Comparing both the equations, A = -A A + A = 0 2A = 0 A = 0 then A is a zero matrix.

question_html: An amount of ₹ 10,000 is put into three investments at the rate of {tex}10 \%{/tex}, {tex}12 \%{/tex} and {tex}15 \%{/tex} per annum. The combined annual income of all three investments is ₹ 1,310 , however the combined annual income of the first and the second investments is ₹ 190 short of the income from the third. Use matrix method and find the investment amount in each at the beginning of the year.
question_text: An amount of ₹ 10,000 is put into three investments at the rate of {tex}10 \%{/tex} , {tex}12 \%{/tex} and {tex}15 \%{/tex} per annum. The combined annual income of all three investments is ₹ 1,310 , however the combined annual income of the first and the second investments is ₹ 190 short of the income from the third. Use matrix method and find the investment amount in each at the beginning of the year.
explanation: Let the numbers are x,y,z x + y + z = 10,000 …(i) Also, 0.1x + 0.12y + 0.15z = 1310 …(ii) Again, 0.1x + 0.12y – 0.15z = – 190 …(iii) {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & 0.15 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & -0.15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 1(– 0.036) – 1(– 0.03) + 1(0) = – 0.006 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A –1 B C 11 = – 0.036 C 12 = 0.27 C 13 = 0 C 21 = 0.27 C 22 = – 0.25 C 23 = – 0.02 C 31 = 0.03 C 32 = – 0.05 C 33 = 0.02 {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.27 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0.03 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{l} -12 \\ -18 \\ -30 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2000 \\ 3000 \\ 5000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = ₹ 2000, y = ₹ 3000 and z = ₹ 5000
solution: Let the numbers are x,y,z x + y + z = 10,000 …(i) Also, 0.1x + 0.12y + 0.15z = 1310 …(ii) Again, 0.1x + 0.12y – 0.15z = – 190 …(iii) {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & 0.15 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & -0.15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 1(– 0.036) – 1(– 0.03) + 1(0) = – 0.006 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A –1 B C 11 = – 0.036 C 12 = 0.27 C 13 = 0 C 21 = 0.27 C 22 = – 0.25 C 23 = – 0.02 C 31 = 0.03 C 32 = – 0.05 C 33 = 0.02 {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.27 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0.03 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{l} -12 \\ -18 \\ -30 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2000 \\ 3000 \\ 5000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = ₹ 2000, y = ₹ 3000 and z = ₹ 5000
solution_html:
Let the numbers are x,y,z
x + y + z = 10,000 …(i)
Also,
0.1x + 0.12y + 0.15z = 1310 …(ii)
Again,
0.1x + 0.12y – 0.15z = – 190 …(iii)
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & 0.15 \\ 0.1 & 0.12 & -0.15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A X = B
|A| = 1(– 0.036) – 1(– 0.03) + 1(0)
= – 0.006
Hence, the unique solution given by x = A–1B
C11 = – 0.036
C12 = 0.27
C13 = 0
C21 = 0.27
C22 = – 0.25
C23 = – 0.02
C31 = 0.03
C32 = – 0.05
C33 = 0.02
{tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex}
{tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.27 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0.03 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -0.036 & 0.27 & 0.03 \\ 0.03 & -0.25 & -0.05 \\ 0 & -0.02 & 0.02 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10000 \\ 1310 \\ -190 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-0.006}\left[\begin{array}{l} -12 \\ -18 \\ -30 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2000 \\ 3000 \\ 5000 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = ₹ 2000, y = ₹ 3000 and z = ₹ 5000

question_html: Find the value of x, if {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&5&1 \\ {15}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}
question_text: Find the value of x, if {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&5&1 \\ {15}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}
explanation: We have, {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&1 \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&5&1 \\ {15}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 2x + 15}&{3 + 5x + 3}&{2 + x + 2} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{/tex} {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {16 + 2x}&{5x + 6}&{x + 4} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {16 + 2x + (5x + 6).2 + (x + 4)x} \right]_{1 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [16 + 2x + 10x + 12 + x 2 + 4x] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x 2 + 16x + 28] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x 2 + 2x + 14x + 28] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 2)(x + 14) = 0 {tex}\therefore {/tex} x = -2, -14
solution: We have, {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&1 \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&5&1 \\ {15}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 2x + 15}&{3 + 5x + 3}&{2 + x + 2} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{/tex} {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {16 + 2x}&{5x + 6}&{x + 4} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {16 + 2x + (5x + 6).2 + (x + 4)x} \right]_{1 \times 1}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [16 + 2x + 10x + 12 + x 2 + 4x] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x 2 + 16x + 28] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x 2 + 2x + 14x + 28] = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 2)(x + 14) = 0 {tex}\therefore {/tex} x = -2, -14
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&1 \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&5&1 \\ {15}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 2x + 15}&{3 + 5x + 3}&{2 + x + 2} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{/tex}{tex}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {16 + 2x}&{5x + 6}&{x + 4} \end{array}} \right]_{1 \times 3}}{\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ x \end{array}} \right]_{3 \times 1}} = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\left[ {16 + 2x + (5x + 6).2 + (x + 4)x} \right]_{1 \times 1}} = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [16 + 2x + 10x + 12 + x2 + 4x] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x2 + 16x + 28] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [x2 + 2x + 14x + 28] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 2)(x + 14) = 0
{tex}\therefore {/tex} x = -2, -14

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
A school wants to allocate students into three clubs : Sports, Music and Drama, under following conditions:

  • The number of students in Sports club should be equal to the sum of the number of students in Music and Drama club.
  • The number of students in Music club should be 20 more than half the number of students in Sports club.
  • The total number of students to be allocated in all three clubs are 180.

Find the number of students allocated to different clubs, using matrix method.
question_text: A school wants to allocate students into three clubs : Sports, Music and Drama, under following conditions: The number of students in Sports club should be equal to the sum of the number of students in Music and Drama club. The number of students in Music club should be 20 more than half the number of students in Sports club. The total number of students to be allocated in all three clubs are 180. Find the number of students allocated to different clubs, using matrix method.
explanation: Let {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} be the no. of students allocated to Sports, Music and Drama clubs respectively. Here, {tex}x=y+z, y=\frac{x}{2}+20, x+y+z=180{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x-y-z=0, x-2 y=-40, x+y+z=180 {/tex} Given equations can be written as {tex}A X=B{/tex} where, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}0 \\ -40 \\ 180\end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|A|=-4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \times \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B {/tex} {tex} =\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -40 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 90 \\ 65 \\ 25 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=90, y=65, z=25 {/tex} Number of students allocated in sports, music and drama are 90,65 and 25 respectively.
solution: Let {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} be the no. of students allocated to Sports, Music and Drama clubs respectively. Here, {tex}x=y+z, y=\frac{x}{2}+20, x+y+z=180{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x-y-z=0, x-2 y=-40, x+y+z=180 {/tex} Given equations can be written as {tex}A X=B{/tex} where, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}0 \\ -40 \\ 180\end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|A|=-4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \times \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B {/tex} {tex} =\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -40 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 90 \\ 65 \\ 25 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=90, y=65, z=25 {/tex} Number of students allocated in sports, music and drama are 90,65 and 25 respectively.
solution_html:
Let {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} be the no. of students allocated to Sports, Music and Drama clubs respectively.
Here, {tex}x=y+z, y=\frac{x}{2}+20, x+y+z=180{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow x-y-z=0, x-2 y=-40, x+y+z=180 {/tex}
Given equations can be written as {tex}A X=B{/tex}
where, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}0 \\ -40 \\ 180\end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}|A|=-4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists.
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \times \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} X=A^{-1} B {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -40 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 90 \\ 65 \\ 25 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \therefore x=90, y=65, z=25 {/tex}
Number of students allocated in sports, music and drama are 90,65 and 25 respectively.

question_html: A furniture workshop produces three types of furniture - chairs, tables and beds each day. On a particular day the total number of furniture pieces produced is 45. It was also found that production of beds exceeds that of chairs by 8, while the total production of beds and chairs together is twice the production of tables. Determine the units produced of each type of furniture, using matrix method.
question_text: A furniture workshop produces three types of furniture - chairs, tables and beds each day. On a particular day the total number of furniture pieces produced is 45. It was also found that production of beds exceeds that of chairs by 8, while the total production of beds and chairs together is twice the production of tables. Determine the units produced of each type of furniture, using matrix method.
explanation: Let the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced be {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} respectively. {tex}\therefore x+y+z=45 ; -x{/tex} {tex}+0 . y+z=8 ; x-2 y+z=0{/tex} Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & 1 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -2 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|{A}|={1}({0}+{2})-{1}(-{1}-{1}){/tex} {tex}+{1}({2}-{0})={6} \neq {0}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A}^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj}({A})=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}11 \\ 15 \\ 19\end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}x=11, y=15, z=19{/tex} Hence the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced are 11, 15 and 19 respectively.
solution: Let the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced be {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} respectively. {tex}\therefore x+y+z=45 ; -x{/tex} {tex}+0 . y+z=8 ; x-2 y+z=0{/tex} Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & 1 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -2 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|{A}|={1}({0}+{2})-{1}(-{1}-{1}){/tex} {tex}+{1}({2}-{0})={6} \neq {0}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A}^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj}({A})=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}11 \\ 15 \\ 19\end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}x=11, y=15, z=19{/tex} Hence the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced are 11, 15 and 19 respectively.
solution_html:
Let the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced be {tex}x, y{/tex} and {tex}z{/tex} respectively.
{tex}\therefore x+y+z=45 ; -x{/tex} {tex}+0 . y+z=8 ; x-2 y+z=0{/tex}
Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & 1 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -2 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}|{A}|={1}({0}+{2})-{1}(-{1}-{1}){/tex} {tex}+{1}({2}-{0})={6} \neq {0}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {A}^{-1}{/tex} exists
{tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex}
{tex}\operatorname{adj}({A})=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 2 & 3 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}45 \\ 8 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}11 \\ 15 \\ 19\end{array}\right]{/tex}
So, {tex}x=11, y=15, z=19{/tex}
Hence the numbers of chairs, tables and beds produced are 11, 15 and 19 respectively.

question_html:
Given {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & 4 & 4 \\ -7 & 1 & 3 \\ 5 & -3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 1 & -2 & -2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find AB . Hence, solve the system of linear equations:
{tex}x-y+z=4{/tex}
{tex}x-2 y-2 z=9{/tex}
{tex}2 x+y+3 z=1{/tex}
question_text: Given {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & 4 & 4 \\ -7 & 1 & 3 \\ 5 & -3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 1 & -2 & -2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find AB . Hence, solve the system of linear equations: {tex}x-y+z=4{/tex} {tex}x-2 y-2 z=9{/tex} {tex}2 x+y+3 z=1{/tex}
explanation: The system of equations is equivalent to the matrix equation: {tex}B X=C{/tex} , where {tex}C=\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=B^{-1} C{/tex} {tex}A B=8 I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow B^{-1}=\frac{1}{8} A{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & 4 & 4 \\ -7 & 1 & 3 \\ 5 & -3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{c}24 \\ -16 \\ -8\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {x}=3, {y}=-2, {z}=-1{/tex}
solution: The system of equations is equivalent to the matrix equation: {tex}B X=C{/tex} , where {tex}C=\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=B^{-1} C{/tex} {tex}A B=8 I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow B^{-1}=\frac{1}{8} A{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & 4 & 4 \\ -7 & 1 & 3 \\ 5 & -3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{c}24 \\ -16 \\ -8\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {x}=3, {y}=-2, {z}=-1{/tex}
solution_html:
The system of equations is equivalent to the matrix equation:
{tex}B X=C{/tex}, where {tex}C=\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow X=B^{-1} C{/tex}
{tex}A B=8 I{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow B^{-1}=\frac{1}{8} A{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & 4 & 4 \\ -7 & 1 & 3 \\ 5 & -3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}4 \\ 9 \\ 1\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{c}24 \\ -16 \\ -8\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {x}=3, {y}=-2, {z}=-1{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then show that {tex}A^{2}-4 A+7 I=0{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then show that {tex}A^{2}-4 A+7 I=0{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} . L.H.S. {tex}=A^{2}-4 A+7 I{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 12 \\ -4 & 8\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}7 & 0 \\ 0 & 7\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=O{/tex} = R.H.S.
solution: {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} . L.H.S. {tex}=A^{2}-4 A+7 I{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 12 \\ -4 & 8\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}7 & 0 \\ 0 & 7\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=O{/tex} = R.H.S.
solution_html:
{tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & 3 \\ -1 & 2\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}.
L.H.S. {tex}=A^{2}-4 A+7 I{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 & 12 \\ -4 & 1\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 12 \\ -4 & 8\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}7 & 0 \\ 0 & 7\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=O{/tex} = R.H.S.

question_html: Three students run on a racing track such that their speeds add up to {tex}6 {~km} / {h}{/tex}. However, double the speed of the third runner added to the speed of the first results in {tex}7 {~km} / {h}{/tex}. If thrice the speed of the first runner is added to the original speeds of the other two, the result is {tex}12 {~km} / {h}{/tex}. Using matrix method, find the original speed of each runner.
question_text: Three students run on a racing track such that their speeds add up to {tex}6 {~km} / {h}{/tex} . However, double the speed of the third runner added to the speed of the first results in {tex}7 {~km} / {h}{/tex} . If thrice the speed of the first runner is added to the original speeds of the other two, the result is {tex}12 {~km} / {h}{/tex} . Using matrix method, find the original speed of each runner.
explanation: Let original speed of three runners be {tex}{x}, {y}{/tex} and z respectively. Then {tex}{x}+{y}+{z}={6} ; {x}+{2 z}={7} ; {3} {x}+{y}+{z}={1 2}{/tex} Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|A|=4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex} {tex}\boldsymbol{\operatorname { a d j }}(A)=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}3 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence the original speed of three runners are {tex}3 {~km} / {h}, 1 {~km} / {h}{/tex} and {tex}2 {~km} / {h}{/tex} respectively.
solution: Let original speed of three runners be {tex}{x}, {y}{/tex} and z respectively. Then {tex}{x}+{y}+{z}={6} ; {x}+{2 z}={7} ; {3} {x}+{y}+{z}={1 2}{/tex} Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}|A|=4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex} {tex}\boldsymbol{\operatorname { a d j }}(A)=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}3 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence the original speed of three runners are {tex}3 {~km} / {h}, 1 {~km} / {h}{/tex} and {tex}2 {~km} / {h}{/tex} respectively.
solution_html:
Let original speed of three runners be {tex}{x}, {y}{/tex} and z respectively.
Then {tex}{x}+{y}+{z}={6} ; {x}+{2 z}={7} ; {3} {x}+{y}+{z}={1 2}{/tex}
Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}|A|=4 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists
{tex}{A X}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-{1}} {B}{/tex}
{tex}\boldsymbol{\operatorname { a d j }}(A)=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}6 \\ 7 \\ 12\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}3 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence the original speed of three runners are {tex}3 {~km} / {h}, 1 {~km} / {h}{/tex} and {tex}2 {~km} / {h}{/tex} respectively.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find BA and use this to solve the system of equations:
{tex}{y}+2 {z}=8{/tex},
{tex}x-y=-1{/tex},
{tex}2 {x}+3 {y}+4 {z}=20{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find BA and use this to solve the system of equations: {tex}{y}+2 {z}=8{/tex} , {tex}x-y=-1{/tex} , {tex}2 {x}+3 {y}+4 {z}=20{/tex}
explanation: {tex}{BA}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 6 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 6 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right]=6 {I}{/tex} Given system is {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}-1 \\ 20 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} i.e., {tex}{BX}={C}{/tex} say so {tex}{X}={B}^{-1} {C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6} A C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 20 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=1, {y}=2, {z}=3{/tex}
solution: {tex}{BA}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 6 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 6 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right]=6 {I}{/tex} Given system is {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}-1 \\ 20 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} i.e., {tex}{BX}={C}{/tex} say so {tex}{X}={B}^{-1} {C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6} A C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 20 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=1, {y}=2, {z}=3{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}{BA}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 6 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 6 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right]=6 {I}{/tex}
Given system is
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}-1 \\ 20 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex}
i.e., {tex}{BX}={C}{/tex} say
so {tex}{X}={B}^{-1} {C}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{6} A C{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 20 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}{x}=1, {y}=2, {z}=3{/tex}

question_html:
Four friends Abhay, Bina, Chhaya and Devesh were asked to simplify {tex}4 {AB}+3({AB}+{BA})-4 {BA}{/tex}, where A and B are both matrices of order {tex}2 \times 2{/tex}. It is known that {tex}A \neq B \neq I{/tex} and {tex}A^{-1} \neq B{/tex}.
Their answers are given as:
Abhay: 6 AB

question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}A^{2}=k A{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}k{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right] \Rightarrow A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=k A \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 k & -2 k \\ -2 k & 2 k \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow k=4{/tex}
solution: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right] \Rightarrow A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=k A \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 k & -2 k \\ -2 k & 2 k \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow k=4{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow k=4{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}1 & 0 \\ -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}K{/tex} if {tex}A^{2}=6 A+K I_{2}{/tex}, where {tex}I_{2}{/tex} is an identity matrix.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}1 & 0 \\ -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}K{/tex} if {tex}A^{2}=6 A+K I_{2}{/tex} , where {tex}I_{2}{/tex} is an identity matrix.
explanation: {tex}A^{2}=6 A+K I_{2} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -6 & 25 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 6+K & 0 \\ -6 & 30+K \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6+K=1 \Rightarrow K=-5, \text { also satisfies } 30+K=25{/tex}
solution: {tex}A^{2}=6 A+K I_{2} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -6 & 25 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 6+K & 0 \\ -6 & 30+K \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6+K=1 \Rightarrow K=-5, \text { also satisfies } 30+K=25{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 6+K=1 \Rightarrow K=-5, \text { also satisfies } 30+K=25{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & -1 & 0\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 0 & 1 \\ -1 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 5 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{C}=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 3 \\ 4\end{array}\right]{/tex}, are three matrices, then find ABC.
question_text: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & -1 & 0\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 0 & 1 \\ -1 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 5 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{C}=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 3 \\ 4\end{array}\right]{/tex} , are three matrices, then find ABC.
explanation: {tex}\text { Required product } ={/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2+1+0 & 0-3+0 & 1-4+0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3-3 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=[-15]{/tex}
solution: {tex}\text { Required product } ={/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2+1+0 & 0-3+0 & 1-4+0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3-3 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=[-15]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\text { Required product } ={/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2+1+0 & 0-3+0 & 1-4+0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3-3 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=[-15]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A is a {tex}3 \times 3{/tex} invertible matrix, show that for any scalar {tex}{k} \neq 0{/tex}, {tex}(k A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{k} A^{-1}{/tex}. Hence calculate {tex}(3 A)^{-1}{/tex}, where {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -1 & 1 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
question_text: If A is a {tex}3 \times 3{/tex} invertible matrix, show that for any scalar {tex}{k} \neq 0{/tex} , {tex}(k A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{k} A^{-1}{/tex} . Hence calculate {tex}(3 A)^{-1}{/tex} , where {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -1 & 1 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
explanation: {tex} \text { Consider }(k A)\left(\frac{1}{k} A^{-1}\right)=k \cdot \frac{1}{k}\left(A \cdot A^{-1}\right)=I {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow k A \text { and } \frac{1}{k} A^{-1} \text { are inverse of each other. } {/tex} {tex} \therefore(k A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{k} A^{-1} {/tex} {tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{3} A^{-1} {/tex} {tex} \text { Here, }|A|=4 \neq 0 \therefore A^{-1} \text { exists. } {/tex} {tex} \text { adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \cdot \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
solution: {tex} \text { Consider }(k A)\left(\frac{1}{k} A^{-1}\right)=k \cdot \frac{1}{k}\left(A \cdot A^{-1}\right)=I {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow k A \text { and } \frac{1}{k} A^{-1} \text { are inverse of each other. } {/tex} {tex} \therefore(k A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{k} A^{-1} {/tex} {tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{3} A^{-1} {/tex} {tex} \text { Here, }|A|=4 \neq 0 \therefore A^{-1} \text { exists. } {/tex} {tex} \text { adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \cdot \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} \text { Consider }(k A)\left(\frac{1}{k} A^{-1}\right)=k \cdot \frac{1}{k}\left(A \cdot A^{-1}\right)=I {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow k A \text { and } \frac{1}{k} A^{-1} \text { are inverse of each other. } {/tex}
{tex} \therefore(k A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{k} A^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{3} A^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Here, }|A|=4 \neq 0 \therefore A^{-1} \text { exists. } {/tex}
{tex} \text { adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \therefore A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \cdot \operatorname{adj} A=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \therefore(3 A)^{-1}=\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 \\ 2 & -3\end{array}\right] {A}=\left[\begin{array}{r}2 \\ -5 \\ -17\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find matrix A.
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 \\ 2 & -3\end{array}\right] {A}=\left[\begin{array}{r}2 \\ -5 \\ -17\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find matrix A.
explanation: {tex}\text { Let } A=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ -5 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 x-y=2, y=-5 \text { and }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=-1{/tex} Put the values in {tex}2 x-3 y=-17{/tex} , L.H.S. {tex}=2(-1)-3(-5) \neq-17={/tex} R.H.S. {tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix ' {tex}A{/tex} ' does not exist.
solution: {tex}\text { Let } A=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ -5 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 x-y=2, y=-5 \text { and }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=-1{/tex} Put the values in {tex}2 x-3 y=-17{/tex} , L.H.S. {tex}=2(-1)-3(-5) \neq-17={/tex} R.H.S. {tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix ' {tex}A{/tex} ' does not exist.
solution_html:
{tex}\text { Let } A=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ -5 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 3 x-y=2, y=-5 \text { and }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=-1{/tex}
Put the values in {tex}2 x-3 y=-17{/tex}, L.H.S. {tex}=2(-1)-3(-5) \neq-17={/tex} R.H.S.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix '{tex}A{/tex}' does not exist.

question_html: Let {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{r}1 \\ 4 \\ -2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{C}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex} be two matrices. Then, find the matrix B if {tex}{AB}={C}{/tex}.
question_text: Let {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{r}1 \\ 4 \\ -2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{C}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex} be two matrices. Then, find the matrix B if {tex}{AB}={C}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex} \text {Let } {B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {x} & {y} & {z} \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex}{AB}={C} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{ccc} x & y & z \\ 4 x & 4 y & 4 z \\ -2 x & -2 y & -2 z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4 \end{array}\right] \text { which }{/tex} {tex}\text { gives } {x}=3, {y}=4 \text { and } {z}=2 {/tex} {tex} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 4 & 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
solution: {tex} \text {Let } {B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {x} & {y} & {z} \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex}{AB}={C} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{ccc} x & y & z \\ 4 x & 4 y & 4 z \\ -2 x & -2 y & -2 z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4 \end{array}\right] \text { which }{/tex} {tex}\text { gives } {x}=3, {y}=4 \text { and } {z}=2 {/tex} {tex} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 4 & 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} \text {Let } {B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {x} & {y} & {z} \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex}{AB}={C} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{ccc} x & y & z \\ 4 x & 4 y & 4 z \\ -2 x & -2 y & -2 z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4 \end{array}\right] \text { which }{/tex}{tex}\text { gives } {x}=3, {y}=4 \text { and } {z}=2 {/tex}
{tex} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 4 & 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: A shopkeeper sells 50 Chemistry, 60 Physics and 35 Maths books on day I and sells 40 Chemistry, 45 Physics and 50 Maths books on day II. If the selling price for each such subject book is ₹ 150 (Chemistry), ₹ 175 (Physics) and ₹ 180 (Maths), then find his total sale in two days, using matrix method. If cost price of all the books together is ₹ 35,000, what profit did he earn after the sale of two days?
question_text: A shopkeeper sells 50 Chemistry, 60 Physics and 35 Maths books on day I and sells 40 Chemistry, 45 Physics and 50 Maths books on day II. If the selling price for each such subject book is ₹ 150 (Chemistry), ₹ 175 (Physics) and ₹ 180 (Maths), then find his total sale in two days, using matrix method. If cost price of all the books together is ₹ 35,000, what profit did he earn after the sale of two days?
explanation: Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned},{/tex} {tex}B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]{/tex} be the day wise sale and the selling price per subject, matrices respectively. Total sales day wise {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 24,300 \\ 22,875 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned}{/tex} Total sales in two days {tex}=₹ 24,300+₹ 22,875=₹ 47,175{/tex} {tex} \text { Profit }=₹ 47,175-₹ 35,000=₹ 12,175 \text {. } {/tex}
solution: Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned},{/tex} {tex}B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]{/tex} be the day wise sale and the selling price per subject, matrices respectively. Total sales day wise {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 24,300 \\ 22,875 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned}{/tex} Total sales in two days {tex}=₹ 24,300+₹ 22,875=₹ 47,175{/tex} {tex} \text { Profit }=₹ 47,175-₹ 35,000=₹ 12,175 \text {. } {/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned},{/tex} {tex}B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]{/tex}be the day wise sale and the selling price per subject, matrices respectively.
Total sales day wise {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 50 & 60 & 35 \\ 40 & 45 & 50 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 150 \\ 175 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 24,300 \\ 22,875 \end{array}\right] \begin{aligned} & \text { Day I } \\ & \text { Day II } \end{aligned}{/tex}
Total sales in two days {tex}=₹ 24,300+₹ 22,875=₹ 47,175{/tex}
{tex} \text { Profit }=₹ 47,175-₹ 35,000=₹ 12,175 \text {. } {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Let {tex}2 x+5 y-1=0{/tex} and {tex}3 x+2 y-7=0{/tex} represent the equations of two lines on which the ants are moving on the ground. Using matrix method, find a point common to the paths of the ants.
question_text: Let {tex}2 x+5 y-1=0{/tex} and {tex}3 x+2 y-7=0{/tex} represent the equations of two lines on which the ants are moving on the ground. Using matrix method, find a point common to the paths of the ants.
explanation: The system of equations in matrices is: {tex}A X=B \text {, where } A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The solution is given by {tex}X=A^{-1} B {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\frac{-1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & -5 \\ -3 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 7\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Point common to paths of the ants is {tex}(3,-1){/tex} .
solution: The system of equations in matrices is: {tex}A X=B \text {, where } A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The solution is given by {tex}X=A^{-1} B {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\frac{-1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & -5 \\ -3 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 7\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Point common to paths of the ants is {tex}(3,-1){/tex} .
solution_html:
The system of equations in matrices is:
{tex}A X=B \text {, where } A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
The solution is given by {tex}X=A^{-1} B {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\frac{-1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 & -5 \\ -3 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 7\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Point common to paths of the ants is {tex}(3,-1){/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Three students, Neha, Rani and Sam go to a market to purchase stationery items. Neha buys 4 pens, 3 notepads and 2 erasers and pays ₹ 60. Rani buys 2 pens, 4 notepads and 6 erasers for ₹ 90. Sam pays ₹ 70 for 6 pens, 2 notepads and 3 erasers.
Based upon the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Form the equations required to solve the problem of finding the price of each item, and express it in the matrix form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex}. (1)
  2. Find {tex}|{A}|{/tex} and confirm if it is possible to find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}. (1)
    1. Find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}, if possible, and write the formula to find X. (2)

      OR

    2. Find {tex}{A}^{2}-8 {I}{/tex}, where I is an identity matrix. (2)
question_text: Three students, Neha, Rani and Sam go to a market to purchase stationery items. Neha buys 4 pens, 3 notepads and 2 erasers and pays ₹ 60. Rani buys 2 pens, 4 notepads and 6 erasers for ₹ 90. Sam pays ₹ 70 for 6 pens, 2 notepads and 3 erasers. Based upon the above information, answer the following questions: Form the equations required to solve the problem of finding the price of each item, and express it in the matrix form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} . (1) Find {tex}|{A}|{/tex} and confirm if it is possible to find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} . (1) Find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} , if possible, and write the formula to find X. (2) OR Find {tex}{A}^{2}-8 {I}{/tex} , where I is an identity matrix. (2)
explanation: Let the price of each pen, notepad, eraser be ₹ {tex}x{/tex} , ₹ {tex}y{/tex} and ₹ {tex}z{/tex} respectively Given system in the form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} is {tex}\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{l}60 \\ 90 \\ 70\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}|{A}|=50 \neq 0{/tex} , hence {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} {A}}{|{A}|}=\frac{1}{50}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -5 & 10 \\ 30 & 0 & -20 \\ -20 & 10 & 10\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}{X}={A}^{-1} {~B}{/tex} OR {tex}A^{2}=\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll}34 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 34 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 33\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}A^{2}-8 I=\left(\begin{array}{lll}26 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 26 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 2.5\end{array}\right){/tex}
solution: Let the price of each pen, notepad, eraser be ₹ {tex}x{/tex} , ₹ {tex}y{/tex} and ₹ {tex}z{/tex} respectively Given system in the form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} is {tex}\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{l}60 \\ 90 \\ 70\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}|{A}|=50 \neq 0{/tex} , hence {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} {A}}{|{A}|}=\frac{1}{50}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -5 & 10 \\ 30 & 0 & -20 \\ -20 & 10 & 10\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}{X}={A}^{-1} {~B}{/tex} OR {tex}A^{2}=\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll}34 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 34 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 33\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}A^{2}-8 I=\left(\begin{array}{lll}26 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 26 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 2.5\end{array}\right){/tex}
solution_html:
1. Let the price of each pen, notepad, eraser be ₹ {tex}x{/tex}, ₹ {tex}y{/tex} and ₹ {tex}z{/tex} respectively
    Given system in the form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} is {tex}\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{l}60 \\ 90 \\ 70\end{array}\right){/tex}
  2. {tex}|{A}|=50 \neq 0{/tex}, hence {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists
    1. {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} {A}}{|{A}|}=\frac{1}{50}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -5 & 10 \\ 30 & 0 & -20 \\ -20 & 10 & 10\end{array}\right){/tex}
      {tex}{X}={A}^{-1} {~B}{/tex}

      OR

    2. {tex}A^{2}=\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{lll}4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 6 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll}34 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 34 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 33\end{array}\right){/tex}
      {tex}A^{2}-8 I=\left(\begin{array}{lll}26 & 28 & 32 \\ 52 & 26 & 46 \\ 46 & 32 & 2.5\end{array}\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -1 & \mathrm{a} & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & \mathrm{x} \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A-1 = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ {~b} & {y} & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find the value of (a + x) - (b + y).
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -1 & \mathrm{a} & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & \mathrm{x} \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A -1 = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ {~b} & {y} & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find the value of (a + x) - (b + y).
explanation: AA -1 = I {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & a & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & x \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ b & y & 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1-8 a+2 b & 1+7 a+2 y & 5-5 a \\ -15+b x & 13+x y & 3 x-9 \\ -5+b & 4+y & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} -5 + b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 5, 5 - 5a = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1 4 + y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -4, 3x - 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3 {tex}\therefore{/tex} (a + x) - (b + y) = (1 + 3) - (5 - 4) = 3
solution: AA -1 = I {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & a & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & x \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ b & y & 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1-8 a+2 b & 1+7 a+2 y & 5-5 a \\ -15+b x & 13+x y & 3 x-9 \\ -5+b & 4+y & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} -5 + b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 5, 5 - 5a = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1 4 + y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -4, 3x - 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3 {tex}\therefore{/tex} (a + x) - (b + y) = (1 + 3) - (5 - 4) = 3
solution_html:
AA-1 = I
{tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & a & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & x \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ b & y & 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex}
{tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1-8 a+2 b & 1+7 a+2 y & 5-5 a \\ -15+b x & 13+x y & 3 x-9 \\ -5+b & 4+y & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex}
-5 + b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 5, 5 - 5a = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1
4 + y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -4, 3x - 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (a + x) - (b + y) = (1 + 3) - (5 - 4) = 3

question_html: If F(x) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos x & -\sin x & 0 \\ \sin x & \cos x & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and [F(x)]2 = F(kx), then the value of k is:
question_text: If F(x) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos x & -\sin x & 0 \\ \sin x & \cos x & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and [F(x)] 2 = F(kx), then the value of k is:

options.explanation: To find {tex}k{/tex} such that {tex}[F(x)]^2=F(k x){/tex} , we compute {tex}[F(x)]^2{/tex} : {tex} [F(x)]^2=F(x) F(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x & -2 \sin x \cos x & 0 \\ 2 \sin x \cos x & \cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=F(2 x){/tex} Thus, {tex}k=2{/tex} . The correct answer is 2.

explanation: To find {tex}k{/tex} such that {tex}[F(x)]^2=F(k x){/tex} , we compute {tex}[F(x)]^2{/tex} : {tex} [F(x)]^2=F(x) F(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x & -2 \sin x \cos x & 0 \\ 2 \sin x \cos x & \cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=F(2 x){/tex} Thus, {tex}k=2{/tex} . The correct answer is 2.

question_text: If for the matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \tan \mathrm{x} & 1 \\ -1 & \tan \mathrm{x} \end{array}\right], \mathrm{A}+\mathrm{A}^{\prime}=2 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{I},{/tex} then the value of {tex}\mathrm{x} \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex} is:

options.explanation: Given the matrix {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\tan x & 1 \\ -1 & \tan x\end{array}\right]{/tex} , we find its transpose {tex}A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\tan x & -1 \\ 1 & \tan x\end{array}\right]{/tex} Setting {tex}A+A^{\prime}=2 \sqrt{3} I{/tex} leads to {tex}2 \tan x=2 \sqrt{3}{/tex} , giving {tex}\tan x=\sqrt{3}{/tex} . Thus, {tex}x={/tex} {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} .

question_html: If A = {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & \cot x \\ -\cot x & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, show that A' A-1 = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rr} -\cos 2 x & -\sin 2 x \\ \sin 2 x & -\cos 2 x \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: If A = {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & \cot x \\ -\cot x & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , show that A' A -1 = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rr} -\cos 2 x & -\sin 2 x \\ \sin 2 x & -\cos 2 x \end{array}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: {tex} |A|=1+\cot ^2 x=\operatorname{cosec}^2 x {/tex} {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \operatorname{cotx} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}={/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime} A^{-1}={/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1-\cot ^2 x & -2 \cot x \\ 2 \cot x & 1-\cot ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x & -2 \sin x \cos x \\ 2 \sin x \cos x & \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -\cos 2 x & -\sin 2 x \\ \sin 2 x & -\cos 2 x \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: {tex} |A|=1+\cot ^2 x=\operatorname{cosec}^2 x {/tex} {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \operatorname{cotx} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}={/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime} A^{-1}={/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1-\cot ^2 x & -2 \cot x \\ 2 \cot x & 1-\cot ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x & -2 \sin x \cos x \\ 2 \sin x \cos x & \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -\cos 2 x & -\sin 2 x \\ \sin 2 x & -\cos 2 x \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} |A|=1+\cot ^2 x=\operatorname{cosec}^2 x {/tex}
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \operatorname{cotx} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}={/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -\cot x \\ \cot x & 1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^{\prime} A^{-1}={/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1-\cot ^2 x & -2 \cot x \\ 2 \cot x & 1-\cot ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x & -2 \sin x \cos x \\ 2 \sin x \cos x & \sin ^2 x-\cos ^2 x \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -\cos 2 x & -\sin 2 x \\ \sin 2 x & -\cos 2 x \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
A scholarship is a sum of money provided to a student to help him or her pay for education. Some students are granted scholarships based on their academic achievements, while others are rewarded based on their financial needs.

Every year a school offers scholarships to girl children and meritorious achievers based on certain criteria. In the session 2022-23, the school offered monthly scholarship of ₹ 3,000 each to some girl students and ₹ 4,000 each to meritorious achievers in academics as well as sports.
In all, 50 students were given the scholarships and monthly expenditure incurred by the school on scholarships was {tex}₹ 1,80,000{/tex}.
Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Express the given information algebraically using matrices. (1)
  2. Check whether the system of matrix equations so obtained is consistent or not. (1)
    1. Find the number of scholarships of each kind given by the school, using matrices. (2)

      OR

    2. Had the amount of scholarship given to each girl child and meritorious student been interchanged, what would be the monthly expenditure incurred by the school? (2)
question_text: A scholarship is a sum of money provided to a student to help him or her pay for education. Some students are granted scholarships based on their academic achievements, while others are rewarded based on their financial needs. Every year a school offers scholarships to girl children and meritorious achievers based on certain criteria. In the session 2022-23, the school offered monthly scholarship of ₹ 3,000 each to some girl students and ₹ 4,000 each to meritorious achievers in academics as well as sports. In all, 50 students were given the scholarships and monthly expenditure incurred by the school on scholarships was {tex}₹ 1,80,000{/tex} . Based on the above information, answer the following questions: Express the given information algebraically using matrices. (1) Check whether the system of matrix equations so obtained is consistent or not. (1) Find the number of scholarships of each kind given by the school, using matrices. (2) OR Had the amount of scholarship given to each girl child and meritorious student been interchanged, what would be the monthly expenditure incurred by the school? (2)
explanation: Let No. of girl child scholarships {tex}={x}{/tex} No. of meritorious achievers {tex}=y{/tex} {tex} x+y=50 {/tex} {tex} 3000 x+4000 y=180000 \text { or } 3 x+4 y=180 {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} \left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right|=1 \neq 0 {/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} system is consistent. {tex} \text { Let } {A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right], {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B} {/tex} {tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & -1 \\ -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 20 \\ 30 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {x}=20, {y}=30 {/tex} OR {tex} \text { Required expenditure } {/tex} {tex}=₹[30(3000)+20(4000)] {/tex} {tex} =₹ 1,70,000 {/tex}
solution: Let No. of girl child scholarships {tex}={x}{/tex} No. of meritorious achievers {tex}=y{/tex} {tex} x+y=50 {/tex} {tex} 3000 x+4000 y=180000 \text { or } 3 x+4 y=180 {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} \left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right|=1 \neq 0 {/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} system is consistent. {tex} \text { Let } {A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right], {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B} {/tex} {tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & -1 \\ -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 20 \\ 30 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {x}=20, {y}=30 {/tex} OR {tex} \text { Required expenditure } {/tex} {tex}=₹[30(3000)+20(4000)] {/tex} {tex} =₹ 1,70,000 {/tex}
solution_html:
1. Let No. of girl child scholarships {tex}={x}{/tex}
    No. of meritorious achievers {tex}=y{/tex}
    {tex} x+y=50 {/tex}
    {tex} 3000 x+4000 y=180000 \text { or } 3 x+4 y=180 {/tex}
    {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]} {/tex}
  2. {tex} \left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right|=1 \neq 0 {/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} system is consistent.
    1. {tex} \text { Let } {A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right], {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
      {tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B} {/tex}
      {tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & -1 \\ -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 180 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 20 \\ 30 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
      {tex} \Rightarrow {x}=20, {y}=30 {/tex}

      OR

    2. {tex} \text { Required expenditure } {/tex} {tex}=₹[30(3000)+20(4000)] {/tex}
      {tex} =₹ 1,70,000 {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Find the product of the matrices {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and hence solve the system of linear equations:
{tex}x+2 y-3 z=-4{/tex}
{tex}2 x+3 y+2 z=2{/tex}
{tex}3 x-3 y-4 z=11{/tex}
question_text: Find the product of the matrices {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} and hence solve the system of linear equations: {tex}x+2 y-3 z=-4{/tex} {tex}2 x+3 y+2 z=2{/tex} {tex}3 x-3 y-4 z=11{/tex}
explanation: {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 67 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 67 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 67 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} Solution of the system of equations is given by: {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=} {/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ -2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=3, y=-2, z=1 {/tex}
solution: {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 67 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 67 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 67 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} Solution of the system of equations is given by: {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=} {/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ -2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=3, y=-2, z=1 {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 67 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 67 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 67 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} 
Solution of the system of equations is given by:
{tex} {\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 3 & -3 & -4 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=} {/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -4 \\ 2 \\ 11 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ -2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right], {/tex}
{tex} \therefore x=3, y=-2, z=1 {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}, if {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}. Hence, solve the following system of equations:
{tex}x+2 y+z=5{/tex}
{tex}2 x+3 y=1{/tex}
{tex}x-y+z=8{/tex}
question_text: Find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} , if {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} . Hence, solve the following system of equations: {tex}x+2 y+z=5{/tex} {tex}2 x+3 y=1{/tex} {tex}x-y+z=8{/tex}
explanation: For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex} , Adjoint of Matrix A is {tex}\|A\|=-6 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex} , Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -3 & -3 \\ -2 & 0 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}5 \\ 1 \\ 8\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{c}-12 \\ 6 \\ -30\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}2 \\ -1 \\ 5\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore x=2, y=-1, z=5{/tex}
solution: For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex} , Adjoint of Matrix A is {tex}\|A\|=-6 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex} , Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -3 & -3 \\ -2 & 0 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}5 \\ 1 \\ 8\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{c}-12 \\ 6 \\ -30\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}2 \\ -1 \\ 5\end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore x=2, y=-1, z=5{/tex}
solution_html:
For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex}, Adjoint of Matrix A is
{tex}\|A\|=-6 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists.
{tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex},
Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & -5 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ -3 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right){/tex}
so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as

{tex}\left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -3 & -3 \\ -2 & 0 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}5 \\ 1 \\ 8\end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{6}\left(\begin{array}{c}-12 \\ 6 \\ -30\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}2 \\ -1 \\ 5\end{array}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore x=2, y=-1, z=5{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations:
{tex}2 x+y-3 z=13{/tex}
{tex}3 x+2 y+z=4{/tex}
{tex}x+2 {y}-{z}=8{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations: {tex}2 x+y-3 z=13{/tex} {tex}3 x+2 y+z=4{/tex} {tex}x+2 {y}-{z}=8{/tex}
explanation: For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right),|A|=-16 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right),{/tex} Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c} 13 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{c} -16 \\ -32 \\ 48 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -3 \end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=2, z=-3{/tex}
solution: For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right),|A|=-16 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists. {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right),{/tex} Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c} 13 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{c} -16 \\ -32 \\ 48 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -3 \end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=2, z=-3{/tex}
solution_html:
For Matrix {tex}A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1\end{array}\right),|A|=-16 \neq 0{/tex} so, {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} exists.
{tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right),{/tex}
Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}-4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1\end{array}\right){/tex}
so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as

{tex}\left(\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right)=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -4 & -5 & 7 \\ 4 & 1 & -11 \\ 4 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c} 13 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right){/tex}  {tex}=\frac{-1}{16}\left(\begin{array}{c} -16 \\ -32 \\ 48 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -3 \end{array}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=2, z=-3{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
An architect is developing a plot of land for a commercial complex. When asked about the dimensions of the plot, he said that if the length is decreased by 25 m and the breadth is increased by 25 m, then its area increases by {tex}625 {~m}^{2}{/tex}. If the length is decreased by 20 m and the breadth is increased by 10 m , then its area decreases by {tex}200 {~m}^{2}{/tex}.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Formulate the linear equations in x and y to represent the given information. (2)
  2. Find the dimensions of the plot of land by matrix method. (2)
question_text: An architect is developing a plot of land for a commercial complex. When asked about the dimensions of the plot, he said that if the length is decreased by 25 m and the breadth is increased by 25 m, then its area increases by {tex}625 {~m}^{2}{/tex} . If the length is decreased by 20 m and the breadth is increased by 10 m , then its area decreases by {tex}200 {~m}^{2}{/tex} . On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions: Formulate the linear equations in x and y to represent the given information. (2) Find the dimensions of the plot of land by matrix method. (2)
explanation: {tex} (x-25)(y+25) =x y+625 \Rightarrow x-y=50 {/tex} {tex} (x-20)(y+10) =x y-200 \Rightarrow x-2 y=0 {/tex} The system of linear equations can be written in matrix form as {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] =\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] } ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}= -\left[\begin{array}{ll}-2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} x=100 {~m}, y=50 {~m} {/tex}
solution: {tex} (x-25)(y+25) =x y+625 \Rightarrow x-y=50 {/tex} {tex} (x-20)(y+10) =x y-200 \Rightarrow x-2 y=0 {/tex} The system of linear equations can be written in matrix form as {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] =\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] } ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}= -\left[\begin{array}{ll}-2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} x=100 {~m}, y=50 {~m} {/tex}
solution_html:
1. {tex} (x-25)(y+25) =x y+625 \Rightarrow x-y=50 {/tex}
    {tex} (x-20)(y+10) =x y-200 \Rightarrow x-2 y=0 {/tex}
  2. The system of linear equations can be written in matrix form as
    {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] =\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] } ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}= -\left[\begin{array}{ll}-2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex} x=100 {~m}, y=50 {~m} {/tex}
