question_html: Given a square matrix {tex}A{/tex} of order 3 such that {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, show that {tex}A^{3}=A^{-1}{/tex}.
question_text: Given a square matrix {tex}A{/tex} of order 3 such that {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , show that {tex}A^{3}=A^{-1}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}{A}^{{3}}={A}^{-1} \Leftrightarrow {A}^{4}={I}{/tex} {tex}A^{4}=A^{2} A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=I{/tex}
solution: {tex}{A}^{{3}}={A}^{-1} \Leftrightarrow {A}^{4}={I}{/tex} {tex}A^{4}=A^{2} A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=I{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}{A}^{{3}}={A}^{-1} \Leftrightarrow {A}^{4}={I}{/tex}
{tex}A^{4}=A^{2} A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=I{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Find a matrix A such that {tex}{A}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also, find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}.
question_text: Find a matrix A such that {tex}{A}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Also, find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex} A =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]^{-1} {/tex} {tex} =-\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -5 \\ 2 & 8 \end{array}\right] {/tex} Also, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{34}\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 5 \\ -2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: {tex} A =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]^{-1} {/tex} {tex} =-\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -5 \\ 2 & 8 \end{array}\right] {/tex} Also, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{34}\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 5 \\ -2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} A =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} =-\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -5 \\ 2 & 8 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
Also, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{34}\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 5 \\ -2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Use the product of matrices {tex}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right){/tex} to solve the following system of equations:
{tex}x+2 y-3 z=6{/tex}
{tex}3 x+2 y-2 z=3{/tex}
{tex}2 x-y+z=2{/tex}
question_text: Use the product of matrices {tex}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right){/tex} to solve the following system of equations: {tex}x+2 y-3 z=6{/tex} {tex}3 x+2 y-2 z=3{/tex} {tex}2 x-y+z=2{/tex}
explanation: {tex}A B=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll} 7 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 7 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 7 \end{array}\right)=7 I{/tex} Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{7} B=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex} \left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{c}7 \\ -35 \\ -35\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}1 \\ -5 \\ -5\end{array}\right) {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=1, y=-5, z=-5{/tex}
solution: {tex}A B=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll} 7 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 7 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 7 \end{array}\right)=7 I{/tex} Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{7} B=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right){/tex} so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as {tex} \left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{c}7 \\ -35 \\ -35\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}1 \\ -5 \\ -5\end{array}\right) {/tex} {tex} \therefore x=1, y=-5, z=-5{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}A B=\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4 \end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{lll} 7 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 7 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 7 \end{array}\right)=7 I{/tex}
Thus, {tex}{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{7} B=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right){/tex}
so, Given equation can be written into a matrix equation as

{tex} \left(\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right)=\frac{1}{7}\left(\begin{array}{c}7 \\ -35 \\ -35\end{array}\right)=\left(\begin{array}{c}1 \\ -5 \\ -5\end{array}\right) {/tex}
{tex} \therefore x=1, y=-5, z=-5{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, prove that A3 - 6A2 + 7A + 2I = 0.
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , prove that A 3 - 6A 2 + 7A + 2I = 0.
solution_html:
L.H.S. = A3 - 6A2 + 7A + 2I
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right] + 7\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} + 2\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 0 + 4}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{2 + 0 + 6} \\ {0 + 0 + 2}&{0 + 4 + 0}&{0 + 2 + 3} \\ {2 + 0 + 6}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{4 + 0 + 9} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 0 + 4}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{2 + 0 + 6} \\ {0 + 0 + 2}&{0 + 4 + 0}&{0 + 2 + 3} \\ {2 + 0 + 6}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{4 + 0 + 9} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&0&{14} \\ 0&{14}&7 \\ {14}&0&{21} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&0&0 \\ 0&2&0 \\ 0&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {5 + 0 + 16}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{10 + 0 + 24} \\ {2 + 0 + 10}&{0 + 8 + 0}&{4 + 4 + 15} \\ {8 + 0 + 26}&{0 + 0 + 0}&{16 + 0 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {30}&0&{48} \\ {12}&{24}&{30} \\ {48}&0&{78} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {7 + 2}&{0 + 0}&{14 + 0} \\ {0 + 0}&{14 + 2}&{7 + 0} \\ {14 + 0}&{0 + 0}&{21 + 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {21}&0&{34} \\ {12}&8&{23} \\ {34}&0&{55} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {30}&0&{48} \\ {12}&{24}&{30} \\ {48}&0&{78} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&0&{14} \\ 0&{16}&7 \\ {14}&0&{23} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {21 - 30}&{0 - 0}&{34 - 48} \\ {12 - 12}&{8 - 24}&{23 - 30} \\ {34 - 48}&{0 - 0}&{55 - 78} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&0&{14} \\ 0&{16}&7 \\ {14}&0&{23} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9}&0&{ - 14} \\ 0&{ - 16}&{ - 7} \\ { - 14}&0&{ - 23} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&0&{14} \\ 0&{16}&7 \\ {14}&0&{23} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9 + 9}&{0 + 0}&{ - 14 + 14} \\ {0 + 0}&{ - 16 + 16}&{ - 7 + 7} \\ { - 14 + 14}&{0 + 0}&{ - 23 + 23} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex} (Zero matrix)
= R.H.S. Proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If  A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&3 \\ 3&{ - 2}&1 \\ 4&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} then show that A3 - 23A - 40I = 0
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&3 \\ 3&{ - 2}&1 \\ 4&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} then show that A 3 - 23A - 40I = 0
solution_html:
{tex}{A^2} = A.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {19}&4&8 \\ 1&{12}&8 \\ {14}&6&{15} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}{A^3} = A.A^2= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&3 \\ 3&{ - 2}&1 \\ 4&2&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {19}&4&8 \\ 1&{12}&8 \\ {14}&6&{15} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {63}&{46}&{69} \\ {69}&{ - 6}&{23} \\ {92}&{46}&{63} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{A^3} - 23A - 40I = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {63}&{46}&{69} \\ {69}&{ - 6}&{23} \\ {92}&{46}&{63} \end{array}} \right] - 23\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&3 \\ 3&{ - 2}&1 \\ 4&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} - 40\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: The product {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a & b \\ -b & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} a & -b \\ b & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} is equal to:
question_text: The product {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a & b \\ -b & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} a & -b \\ b & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} is equal to:

options.explanation: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} a & b \\ -b & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{rr} a & -b \\ b & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} a \times a+b \times b & a \times(-b)+b \times a \\ (-b) \times a+a \times b & (-b) \times(-b)+a \times a \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} a^2+b^2 & -a b+a b \\ -a b+a b & b^2+a^2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2 & 0 \\ 0 & \mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_html: If f({tex}\alpha{/tex}) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, prove that f({tex}\alpha{/tex}){tex} \cdot {/tex}f({tex}-\beta{/tex}) = f({tex}\alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex})
question_text: If f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} ) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , prove that f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} ) {tex} \cdot {/tex} f( {tex}-\beta{/tex} ) = f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex} )
explanation: LHS = f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} ) f( {tex}-\beta{/tex} ) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \beta & \sin \beta & 0 \\ -\sin \beta & \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha \cos \beta+\sin \alpha \sin \beta & \cos \alpha \sin \beta-\sin \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ \sin \alpha \cos \beta-\cos \alpha \sin \beta & \sin \alpha \sin \beta+\cos \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos (\alpha-\beta) & -\sin (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ \sin (\alpha-\beta) & \cos (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex} ) = RHS
solution: LHS = f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} ) f( {tex}-\beta{/tex} ) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \beta & \sin \beta & 0 \\ -\sin \beta & \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha \cos \beta+\sin \alpha \sin \beta & \cos \alpha \sin \beta-\sin \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ \sin \alpha \cos \beta-\cos \alpha \sin \beta & \sin \alpha \sin \beta+\cos \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos (\alpha-\beta) & -\sin (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ \sin (\alpha-\beta) & \cos (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = f( {tex}\alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex} ) = RHS
solution_html:
LHS = f({tex}\alpha{/tex}) f({tex}-\beta{/tex}) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \beta & \sin \beta & 0 \\ -\sin \beta & \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha \cos \beta+\sin \alpha \sin \beta & \cos \alpha \sin \beta-\sin \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ \sin \alpha \cos \beta-\cos \alpha \sin \beta & \sin \alpha \sin \beta+\cos \alpha \cos \beta & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos (\alpha-\beta) & -\sin (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ \sin (\alpha-\beta) & \cos (\alpha-\beta) & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= f({tex}\alpha{/tex}-{tex}\beta{/tex}) = RHS

subject: Mathematics
question_html: For what value of {tex}\text x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex}, is A + A' = {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex} I, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \text x & \sin \text x \\ -\sin \text x & \cos \text x \end{array}\right]{/tex}?
question_text: For what value of {tex}\text x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex} , is A + A' = {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex} I, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \text x & \sin \text x \\ -\sin \text x & \cos \text x \end{array}\right]{/tex} ?

options.explanation: {tex} A+A^{\prime}=\sqrt{3} I {/tex} {tex} A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & \sin x \\ -\sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} \sin x & -\sin x \\ \sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A+A^{\prime}=\sqrt{3} I \text {. } {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & \sin x \\ -\sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & -\sin x \\ \sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right]=\sqrt{3}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x+\cos x & 0 \\ 0 & \cos x+\cos x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sqrt{3} & 0 \\ 0 & \sqrt{3} \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 \cos x & 0 \\ 0 & 2 \cos x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sqrt{3} & 0 \\ 0 & \sqrt{3} \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2 \cos x=\sqrt{3} . {/tex} {tex} \cos x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} {/tex} {tex} \cos x=\cos \frac{\pi}{6} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}

explanation: {tex} A+A^{\prime}=\sqrt{3} I {/tex} {tex} A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & \sin x \\ -\sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{rr} \sin x & -\sin x \\ \sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A+A^{\prime}=\sqrt{3} I \text {. } {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & \sin x \\ -\sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x & -\sin x \\ \sin x & \cos x \end{array}\right]=\sqrt{3}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos x+\cos x & 0 \\ 0 & \cos x+\cos x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sqrt{3} & 0 \\ 0 & \sqrt{3} \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 \cos x & 0 \\ 0 & 2 \cos x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} \sqrt{3} & 0 \\ 0 & \sqrt{3} \end{array}\right]} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2 \cos x=\sqrt{3} . {/tex} {tex} \cos x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} {/tex} {tex} \cos x=\cos \frac{\pi}{6} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}\mathrm{x}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]+\mathrm{y}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{4}{9}\right]{/tex}, then:
question_text: If {tex}\mathrm{x}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]+\mathrm{y}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{4}{9}\right]{/tex} , then:

options.explanation: {tex} x\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]+y\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{c} x \\ 2 x \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 y \\ 5 y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x+2 y=4{/tex} ...(i) {tex} 2 x+5 y=9{/tex} ...(ii) form (1) & (2) we get y = 1 Now {tex}x+2 y=4{/tex} {tex}x+2=4{/tex} {tex}x=2 {/tex} {tex}\text { i.e. } x=2 y=1 . {/tex}

explanation: {tex} x\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]+y\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{c} x \\ 2 x \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 y \\ 5 y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x+2 y=4{/tex} ...(i) {tex} 2 x+5 y=9{/tex} ...(ii) form (1) & (2) we get y = 1 Now {tex}x+2 y=4{/tex} {tex}x+2=4{/tex} {tex}x=2 {/tex} {tex}\text { i.e. } x=2 y=1 . {/tex}

question_html: It is given that X {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}. Then matrix X is:
question_text: It is given that X {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} . Then matrix X is:

options.explanation: {tex} x \cdot\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \text { Let } x=\left[\begin{array}{ll} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right] ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[\begin{array}{ll} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}3 a+b & 2 a-b \\ 3 c+d & 2 a-d\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 3a + b = 4 ...(i) 2a - b = 1 ...(ii) 3c + d = 2 ...(iii) 2c - d = 3 ...(iv) from (i) & (ii) [a = 1] 2a - b = 1 2 - b = 1 -b = 1 - 2 -b = -1 [b = 1] Hence {tex}x = \left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

explanation: {tex} x \cdot\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \text { Let } x=\left[\begin{array}{ll} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right] ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left[\begin{array}{ll} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right] } {/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}3 a+b & 2 a-b \\ 3 c+d & 2 a-d\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 3a + b = 4 ...(i) 2a - b = 1 ...(ii) 3c + d = 2 ...(iii) 2c - d = 3 ...(iv) from (i) & (ii) [a = 1] 2a - b = 1 2 - b = 1 -b = 1 - 2 -b = -1 [b = 1] Hence {tex}x = \left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & \mathrm{x} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then x is:
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & \mathrm{x} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then x is:

options.explanation: {tex} \text { If }\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 x+2 \\ x+x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 x+2=14 {/tex} {tex}3 x=12 {/tex} {tex}x=4 {/tex} and {tex}2 x=8{/tex} x = 4

explanation: {tex} \text { If }\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 x+2 \\ x+x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 14 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 x+2=14 {/tex} {tex}3 x=12 {/tex} {tex}x=4 {/tex} and {tex}2 x=8{/tex} x = 4

question_html: In the matrix equation {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} \text x+\text y+\text z \\ \text x+\text z \\ \text y+2 \text z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, the value of z is:
question_text: In the matrix equation {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} \text x+\text y+\text z \\ \text x+\text z \\ \text y+2 \text z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , the value of z is:

question_text: If the matrix A is both symmetric and skew symmetric, then

options.explanation: Only a null matrix can be symmetric as well as skew symmetric. In Symmetric Matrix A T = A, Skew Symmetric Matrix A T = -A, Given that the matrix is satisfying both the properties. Therefore, Equating the RHS we get A = -A i.e, 2A = 0. Therefore A = 0, which is a null matrix.

question_text: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}3 c+6 & a-d \\ a+d & 2-3 b\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}12 & 2 \\ -8 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex} are equal, then value of ab - cd is:

options.explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 c+6 & a-d \\ a+d & 2-3 b \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[\begin{array}{cc} 12 & 2 \\ -8 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} 3c + 6 = 12 ...(i) a - d = 2 ...(ii) a + d = -8 ...(iii) 2 - 3b = -4 ...(iv) From eq. (i), we get c = 2 On solving eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get a = -3 and d = -5 from eq. (iv), we get b = 2 Now, ab - cd = (-3)2 - 2(-5) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ab - cd = -6 + 10 = 4

explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 c+6 & a-d \\ a+d & 2-3 b \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[\begin{array}{cc} 12 & 2 \\ -8 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} 3c + 6 = 12 ...(i) a - d = 2 ...(ii) a + d = -8 ...(iii) 2 - 3b = -4 ...(iv) From eq. (i), we get c = 2 On solving eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get a = -3 and d = -5 from eq. (iv), we get b = 2 Now, ab - cd = (-3)2 - 2(-5) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ab - cd = -6 + 10 = 4

explanation: Matrix A is symmetric {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 = 6 - 5x {tex}\therefore{/tex} x 2 ​ + 5x - 6 = 0 ​​​​​​ {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 6)(x - 1) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -6, 1

A 11 = 6 A 12 = -3 A 13 = -2 A 21 = -28 A 22 = 16 A 23 = 10 A 31 = -16 A 32 = 9 A 33 = 6 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{~A}|} \cdot \operatorname{adj}(\mathrm{A}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -28 & -16 \\ -3 & 16 & 9 \\ -2 & 10 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The given system of equations can be written as A.Z = B where, X = A -1 .B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -28 & -16 \\ -3 & 16 & 9 \\ -2 & 10 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 8 \\ 3 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{c} -2 \\ 3 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -2, y = 3, z = 1

co-factors of the elements of the matrix.

question_html: If A-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B ={tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find (AB)-1.
question_text: If A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find (AB) -1 .
explanation: |B| = 1, adj {tex}\text { (B) }=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}B^{-1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{|B|}{/tex} adj (B) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} (AB) -1 = B -1 . A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 5 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: |B| = 1, adj {tex}\text { (B) }=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}B^{-1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{|B|}{/tex} adj (B) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} (AB) -1 = B -1 . A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 5 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
|B| = 1, adj {tex}\text { (B) }=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}B^{-1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{|B|}{/tex}adj (B) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
(AB)-1 = B-1. A-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 5 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&0&{ - 1} \\ 1&2&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, then show that A3 - 4A2 - 3A + 11I = O, Hence find A-1.
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3&2 \\ 2&0&{ - 1} \\ 1&2&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , then show that A 3 - 4A 2 - 3A + 11I = O, Hence find A -1 .
explanation: {tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^3=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]{/tex} LHS = A 3 - 4A 2 - 3A + 11I {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]=4\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}-3\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right]+11\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=0{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex}-\frac {1}{11}{/tex} (A 2 - 4A - 3I) {tex}=-\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -5 & -3 \\ -7 & 1 & 5 \\ 4 & 1 & -6\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 5 & 3 \\ 7 & -1 & -5 \\ -4 & -1 & 6\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: {tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^3=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]{/tex} LHS = A 3 - 4A 2 - 3A + 11I {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]=4\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}-3\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right]+11\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=0{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex}-\frac {1}{11}{/tex} (A 2 - 4A - 3I) {tex}=-\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -5 & -3 \\ -7 & 1 & 5 \\ 4 & 1 & -6\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 5 & 3 \\ 7 & -1 & -5 \\ -4 & -1 & 6\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^3=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
LHS = A3 - 4A2 - 3A + 11I
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & 37 & 26 \\ 10 & 5 & 1 \\ 35 & 42 & 34 \end{array}\right]=4\left[\begin{array}{lll} 9 & 7 & 5 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \\ 8 & 9 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}-3\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right]+11\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=0{/tex}
Now,
A-1 = {tex}-\frac {1}{11}{/tex}(A2 - 4A - 3I)
{tex}=-\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}2 & -5 & -3 \\ -7 & 1 & 5 \\ 4 & 1 & -6\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-2 & 5 & 3 \\ 7 & -1 & -5 \\ -4 & -1 & 6\end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_text: If A is a square matrix such that A 2 = A, then (I - A) 3 + A is equal to:

options.explanation: Given that A 2 = A Calculating value of (I - A) 3 + A: (I - A) 3 + A = I 3 - 3I 2 A + 3IA 2 - A 3 +A = I - A 2 A - 3A + 3A 2 + A ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} I n = I and IA = A) = I - AA - 3A + 3A + A ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = A) = I + A 2 - 3A + 3A + A = I Hence, (I - A) 3 + A = {tex}I{/tex}

explanation: Given that A 2 = A Calculating value of (I - A) 3 + A: (I - A) 3 + A = I 3 - 3I 2 A + 3IA 2 - A 3 +A = I - A 2 A - 3A + 3A 2 + A ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} I n = I and IA = A) = I - AA - 3A + 3A + A ( {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = A) = I + A 2 - 3A + 3A + A = I Hence, (I - A) 3 + A = {tex}I{/tex}

question_html: If A = [2 -3 4], B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = [1 2 3] and Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex},  then AB + XY equals:
question_text: If A = [2 -3 4], B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = [1 2 3] and Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then AB + XY equals:

options.explanation: AB = [2 -3 4], B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = [6 + (-6) + 8] = [8] XY = [1, 2, 3] and Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = [2 + 6 + 12] = [20] AB + XY = [8] + [20] = [28]

explanation: AB = [2 -3 4], B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = [6 + (-6) + 8] = [8] XY = [1, 2, 3] and Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 3 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = [2 + 6 + 12] = [20] AB + XY = [8] + [20] = [28]

question_html: If [x 1]{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = 0, then x equals
question_text: If [x 1 ] {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = 0, then x equals

options.explanation: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x-2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 2 = 0 [By def. of equality] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2

explanation: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x-2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 2 = 0 [By def. of equality] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the value of x and y are respectively, if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & y \\ 3 y & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find the value of x and y are respectively, if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & y \\ 3 y & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} .

options.explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} x & y \\ 3 y & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} On multiplying the matrices of left side, we get {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} x \cdot 1+2 \cdot y \\ 3 y \cdot 1+2 \cdot x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[\begin{array}{c} x+2 y \\ 3 y+2 x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, equating the corresponding elements, we get x + 2y = 3 ...(i) and 3y + 2x = 5 ...(ii) On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get x = 1 and y = 1

explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} x & y \\ 3 y & x \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} On multiplying the matrices of left side, we get {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} x \cdot 1+2 \cdot y \\ 3 y \cdot 1+2 \cdot x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[\begin{array}{c} x+2 y \\ 3 y+2 x \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, equating the corresponding elements, we get x + 2y = 3 ...(i) and 3y + 2x = 5 ...(ii) On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get x = 1 and y = 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Express A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & -3 \\ 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} as a sum of a symmetric and a skew symmetric matrix.
question_text: Express A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & -3 \\ 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} as a sum of a symmetric and a skew symmetric matrix.
explanation: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 2 \\ -3 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{\text A+\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 -1 \\ -1 -2 \end{array}\right] \\{/tex} Q = {tex}\frac{\text A-\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -5 \\ 5 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A = P + Q P + Q = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 8 -6 \\ 4 -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = A
solution: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 2 \\ -3 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{\text A+\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 -1 \\ -1 -2 \end{array}\right] \\{/tex} Q = {tex}\frac{\text A-\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -5 \\ 5 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A = P + Q P + Q = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 8 -6 \\ 4 -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = A
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AT = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 2 \\ -3 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
P = {tex}\frac{\text A+\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 -1 \\ -1 -2 \end{array}\right] \\{/tex}
Q = {tex}\frac{\text A-\text A^\text T}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -5 \\ 5 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, A = P + Q
P + Q = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 8 -6 \\ 4 -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 -3 \\ 2 -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = A

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find (AB)-1 if A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -4 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find (AB) -1 if A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 0 \\ -4 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 B -1 A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & \frac{1}{2} \\ 9 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 B -1 A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & \frac{1}{2} \\ 9 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
(AB)-1 = B-1A-1
B-1A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\ 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & \frac{1}{2} \\ 9 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the following system of equations by matrix method :
x - y + 2z = 7
2x - y + 3z = 12
3x + 2y - z = 5
question_text: Solve the following system of equations by matrix method : x - y + 2z = 7 2x - y + 3z = 12 3x + 2y - z = 5
explanation: Writing given equations in matrix form {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 3 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Which is of the form AX = B Here |A| = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3
solution: Writing given equations in matrix form {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 3 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Which is of the form AX = B Here |A| = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3
solution_html:
Writing given equations in matrix form
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 3 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Which is of the form AX = B
Here |A| = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A-1B = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 3 & -1 \\ 11 & -7 & 1 \\ 7 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 7 \\ 12 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the matrix A such that A{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find the matrix A such that A {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 4 \\ -1 & 6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 5 & -1 & 4 \\ 2 & 3 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of equations:
5x - y + 4z = 5
2x + 3y + 5z = 2
5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 5 & -1 & 4 \\ 2 & 3 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and use it to solve the following system of equations: 5x - y + 4z = 5 2x + 3y + 5z = 2 5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
explanation: {tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex} {tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex} {tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}
solution: {tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex} {tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex} {tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex}
{tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex}
{tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex}
{tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex}
{tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex}
{tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find scalar k so that A2 + I = kA.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find scalar k so that A 2 + I = kA.
explanation: {tex} A^2+I=K A {/tex} {tex} A^2=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 9+2 & -6-2 \\ -3-1 & 2+1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^2+I=K A .{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=k\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 12 & -8 \\ -4 & 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 k & 2 k \\ k & -k \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 12=-3 k . {/tex} {tex} 3 k=-12 {/tex} {tex} k=-4{/tex}
solution: {tex} A^2+I=K A {/tex} {tex} A^2=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 9+2 & -6-2 \\ -3-1 & 2+1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^2+I=K A .{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=k\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 12 & -8 \\ -4 & 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 k & 2 k \\ k & -k \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 12=-3 k . {/tex} {tex} 3 k=-12 {/tex} {tex} k=-4{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} A^2+I=K A {/tex}
{tex} A^2=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 9+2 & -6-2 \\ -3-1 & 2+1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^2+I=K A .{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 11 & -8 \\ -4 & 3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=k\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 12 & -8 \\ -4 & 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 k & 2 k \\ k & -k \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 12=-3 k . {/tex}
{tex} 3 k=-12 {/tex}
{tex} k=-4{/tex}

question_html: Find the value of x - y, if {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {3} \\ {0} & {x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
question_text: Find the value of x - y, if {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {3} \\ {0} & {x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
explanation: Given: {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {3} \\ {0} & {x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {6} \\ {0} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2+y} & {6} \\ {1} & {2 x+2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Therefore, we have, 2 + y = 5 y = 3 And, 2x + 2 = 8 2x = 6 x = 3 x - y = 3 - 3 Hence, x - y = 0
solution: Given: {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {3} \\ {0} & {x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {6} \\ {0} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2+y} & {6} \\ {1} & {2 x+2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Therefore, we have, 2 + y = 5 y = 3 And, 2x + 2 = 8 2x = 6 x = 3 x - y = 3 - 3 Hence, x - y = 0
solution_html:
Given: {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {3} \\ {0} & {x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {6} \\ {0} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc}{y} & {0} \\ {1} & {2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2+y} & {6} \\ {1} & {2 x+2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{5} & {6} \\ {1} & {8}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Therefore, we have,
2 + y = 5
y = 3
And, 2x + 2 = 8
2x = 6
x = 3
x - y = 3 - 3
Hence, x - y = 0

question_html: Find the value of (x - y) from the matrix equation {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 5 \\ 7 & y-3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
question_text: Find the value of (x - y) from the matrix equation {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 5 \\ 7 & y-3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
explanation: Getting {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}2 x+3 & 6 \\ 15 & 2 y-4\end{array}\right){/tex} = {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14\end{array}\right){/tex} 2x + 3 = 7 and 2y - 4 = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 9.
solution: Getting {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}2 x+3 & 6 \\ 15 & 2 y-4\end{array}\right){/tex} = {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14\end{array}\right){/tex} 2x + 3 = 7 and 2y - 4 = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 9.
solution_html:
Getting {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}2 x+3 & 6 \\ 15 & 2 y-4\end{array}\right){/tex} = {tex}\left(\begin{array}{cc}7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14\end{array}\right){/tex}
2x + 3 = 7 and 2y - 4 = 14 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 9.

question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find A-1. Hence solve the following system of equations: 2x - 3y + 5z = 11, 3x + 2y - 4z = -5, x + y - 2z = -3.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find A -1 . Hence solve the following system of equations: 2x - 3y + 5z = 11, 3x + 2y - 4z = -5, x + y - 2z = -3.
explanation: According to the question, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= 2(-4 + 4)+ 3(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2){/tex} {tex}= - 6 + 5 = - 1{/tex} {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 The cofactors of element of |A| are A 1 1 = 0, A 21 = -1, A 31 = 2 A 12 = 2, A 2 2 = -9, A 32 = 23 A 13 = 1, A 23 = -5, A 33 = 13 Now, adj(A) = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 9 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { 23 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \operatorname { adj } ( A ){/tex} {tex}= ( - 1 ) \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} ......(i) Now, consider the given system of linear equations {tex}2x - 3y + 5z = 11 \\ 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 \\ x + y - 2z = -3{/tex} which can be written in matrix form as AX = B.....(ii) where, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Since, A -1 exists, therefore solution of Eq.(ii) is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} [From Eq.(i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { - 5 + 6 } \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69 } \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 1 } \\ { 2 } \\ { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On equating the corresponding elements, we get {tex}x = 1, y = 2 \ and \ z = 3.{/tex}
solution: According to the question, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= 2(-4 + 4)+ 3(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2){/tex} {tex}= - 6 + 5 = - 1{/tex} {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 The cofactors of element of |A| are A 1 1 = 0, A 21 = -1, A 31 = 2 A 12 = 2, A 2 2 = -9, A 32 = 23 A 13 = 1, A 23 = -5, A 33 = 13 Now, adj(A) = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 9 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { 23 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \operatorname { adj } ( A ){/tex} {tex}= ( - 1 ) \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} ......(i) Now, consider the given system of linear equations {tex}2x - 3y + 5z = 11 \\ 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 \\ x + y - 2z = -3{/tex} which can be written in matrix form as AX = B.....(ii) where, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Since, A -1 exists, therefore solution of Eq.(ii) is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} [From Eq.(i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { - 5 + 6 } \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69 } \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 1 } \\ { 2 } \\ { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On equating the corresponding elements, we get {tex}x = 1, y = 2 \ and \ z = 3.{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= 2(-4 + 4)+ 3(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2){/tex}
{tex}= - 6 + 5 = - 1{/tex}{tex}\ne{/tex} 0
The cofactors of element of |A| are
A11 = 0, A21 = -1, A31 = 2
A12 = 2, A22 = -9, A32 = 23
A13 = 1, A23 = -5, A33 = 13
Now, adj(A) = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 9 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { 23 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \operatorname { adj } ( A ){/tex}
{tex}= ( - 1 ) \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 9 } & { 23 } \\ { 1 } & { - 5 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}......(i)
Now, consider the given system of linear equations
{tex}2x - 3y + 5z = 11 \\ 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 \\ x + y - 2z = -3{/tex}
which can be written in matrix form as AX = B.....(ii)
where, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Since, A-1 exists, therefore solution of Eq.(ii) is given by X = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 9 } & { - 23 } \\ { - 1 } & { 5 } & { - 13 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 11 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}[From Eq.(i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { - 5 + 6 } \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69 } \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39 } \end{array} \right] {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 1 } \\ { 2 } \\ { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
On equating the corresponding elements, we get
{tex}x = 1, y = 2 \ and \ z = 3.{/tex}

question_html: Find a matrix A such that 2A - 3B + 5C = O, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} and C = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find a matrix A such that 2A - 3B + 5C = O, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} and C = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: We have, 2A - 3B + 5C = O {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3B - 5C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - 5 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-6} & {6} & {0} \\ {9} & {3} & {12}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-10} & {0} & {10} \\ {-35} & {-5} & {-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-6-10} & {6+0} & {0+10} \\ {9-35} & {3-5} & {12-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-8} & {3} & {5} \\ {-13} & {-1} & {-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: We have, 2A - 3B + 5C = O {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3B - 5C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - 5 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-6} & {6} & {0} \\ {9} & {3} & {12}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-10} & {0} & {10} \\ {-35} & {-5} & {-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-6-10} & {6+0} & {0+10} \\ {9-35} & {3-5} & {12-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-8} & {3} & {5} \\ {-13} & {-1} & {-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have,
2A - 3B + 5C = O
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3B - 5C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = 3{tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - 5{tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-6} & {6} & {0} \\ {9} & {3} & {12}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-10} & {0} & {10} \\ {-35} & {-5} & {-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-6-10} & {6+0} & {0+10} \\ {9-35} & {3-5} & {12-30}\end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{-16} & {6} & {10} \\ {-26} & {-2} & {-18}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-8} & {3} & {5} \\ {-13} & {-1} & {-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find (A2 - 5A)
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find (A 2 - 5A)
explanation: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=A \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right] \cdot\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2 \times 2+0 \times 2+1 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+0 \times 1+1 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+0 \times 3+1 \times 0} \\ {2 \times 2+1 \times 2+3 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+1 \times 1+3 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+1 \times 3+3 \times 0} \\ {1 \times 2+-1 \times 2+0 \times 1} & {1 \times 0+-1 \times 1+0 \times-1} & {1 \times 1+-1 \times 3+0 \times 0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{10} & {0} & {5} \\ {10} & {5} & {15} \\ {5} & {-5} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5-10} & {-1-0} & {2-5} \\ {9-10} & {-2-5} & {5-15} \\ {0-5} & {-1+5} & {-2-0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-5} & {-1} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {-7} & {-10} \\ {-5} & {4} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=A \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right] \cdot\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2 \times 2+0 \times 2+1 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+0 \times 1+1 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+0 \times 3+1 \times 0} \\ {2 \times 2+1 \times 2+3 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+1 \times 1+3 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+1 \times 3+3 \times 0} \\ {1 \times 2+-1 \times 2+0 \times 1} & {1 \times 0+-1 \times 1+0 \times-1} & {1 \times 1+-1 \times 3+0 \times 0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{10} & {0} & {5} \\ {10} & {5} & {15} \\ {5} & {-5} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5-10} & {-1-0} & {2-5} \\ {9-10} & {-2-5} & {5-15} \\ {0-5} & {-1+5} & {-2-0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-5} & {-1} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {-7} & {-10} \\ {-5} & {4} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}=A \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right] \cdot\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {0} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-1} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2 \times 2+0 \times 2+1 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+0 \times 1+1 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+0 \times 3+1 \times 0} \\ {2 \times 2+1 \times 2+3 \times 1} & {2 \times 0+1 \times 1+3 \times-1} & {2 \times 1+1 \times 3+3 \times 0} \\ {1 \times 2+-1 \times 2+0 \times 1} & {1 \times 0+-1 \times 1+0 \times-1} & {1 \times 1+-1 \times 3+0 \times 0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {-1} & {2} \\ {9} & {-2} & {5} \\ {0} & {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{10} & {0} & {5} \\ {10} & {5} & {15} \\ {5} & {-5} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5-10} & {-1-0} & {2-5} \\ {9-10} & {-2-5} & {5-15} \\ {0-5} & {-1+5} & {-2-0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}-5 A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-5} & {-1} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {-7} & {-10} \\ {-5} & {4} & {-2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A is a square matrix satisfying A'A = 1, write the value of |A|.
question_text: If A is a square matrix satisfying A'A = 1, write the value of |A|.
explanation: Let A = [a ij ] be a square matrix of order n Here, |A| = |A'|[By property of determinants] Given: A' A = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A' A| = 1 Then, |A' A| = |A'||A| [since the determinants are of the same order] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A'||A| = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{\left|A'\right|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{|A|}\left[\therefore|A|=\left|A'\right|\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| 2 =1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= {tex}\pm{/tex} 1
solution: Let A = [a ij ] be a square matrix of order n Here, |A| = |A'|[By property of determinants] Given: A' A = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A' A| = 1 Then, |A' A| = |A'||A| [since the determinants are of the same order] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A'||A| = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{\left|A'\right|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{|A|}\left[\therefore|A|=\left|A'\right|\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| 2 =1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= {tex}\pm{/tex} 1
solution_html:
Let A = [aij] be a square matrix of order n
Here,
|A| = |A'|[By property of determinants]
Given: A' A = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}|A' A| = 1
Then,
|A' A| = |A'||A| [since the determinants are of the same order]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}|A'||A| = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{\left|A'\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|A|=\frac{1}{|A|}\left[\therefore|A|=\left|A'\right|\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}|A|2=1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}|A|= {tex}\pm{/tex}1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, show that (A - 2I) (A - 3I) = 0.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , show that (A - 2I) (A - 3I) = 0.
explanation: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right] {/tex} and {tex}1=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} A - 2I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-2\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4-2} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {0-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} A - 3I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-3\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4-3} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {1-3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} L.H.S. = (A - 2I)(A - 3I) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2-2} & {4-4} \\ {-1+1} & {-2+2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{0} & {0} \\ {0} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = 0 = R.H.S.
solution: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right] {/tex} and {tex}1=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} A - 2I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-2\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4-2} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {0-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} A - 3I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-3\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4-3} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {1-3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} L.H.S. = (A - 2I)(A - 3I) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2-2} & {4-4} \\ {-1+1} & {-2+2}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{0} & {0} \\ {0} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = 0 = R.H.S.
solution_html:
{tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right] {/tex} and {tex}1=\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
A - 2I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-2\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4-2} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {0-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
A - 3I = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{4} & {2} \\ {-1} & {1}\end{array}\right]-3\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr}{4-3} & {2-0} \\ {-1-0} & {1-3}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
L.H.S. = (A - 2I)(A - 3I)
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-1}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc}{1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {-2}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}{2-2} & {4-4} \\ {-1+1} & {-2+2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{0} & {0} \\ {0} & {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}= 0 = R.H.S.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 2 \\ 3 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and kA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 3 a \\ 2 b & 24 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the values of k, a and b.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 2 \\ 3 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and kA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 3 a \\ 2 b & 24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find the values of k, a and b.
explanation: Here, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{kA}=\mathrm{k} \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {3 \mathrm{a}} \\ {2 \mathrm{b}} & {24}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2k = 3a, 3k = 2b and - 4k = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = -6, {tex}a=\frac{-12}{3}=-4{/tex} , {tex}\mathrm{b}=\frac{-18}{2}=-9{/tex}
solution: Here, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{kA}=\mathrm{k} \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {3 \mathrm{a}} \\ {2 \mathrm{b}} & {24}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2k = 3a, 3k = 2b and - 4k = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = -6, {tex}a=\frac{-12}{3}=-4{/tex} , {tex}\mathrm{b}=\frac{-18}{2}=-9{/tex}
solution_html:
Here, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{kA}=\mathrm{k} \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4}\end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {2 \mathrm{k}} \\ {3 \mathrm{k}} & {-4 \mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}{0} & {3 \mathrm{a}} \\ {2 \mathrm{b}} & {24}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2k = 3a, 3k = 2b and - 4k = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  k = -6, {tex}a=\frac{-12}{3}=-4{/tex}, {tex}\mathrm{b}=\frac{-18}{2}=-9{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A and B are symmetric matrices, such that AB and BA are both defined, then prove that AB – BA is a skew symmetric matrix.
question_text: If A and B are symmetric matrices, such that AB and BA are both defined, then prove that AB – BA is a skew symmetric matrix.
explanation: We are Given: A' = A, B' = B (AB - BA) ' = (AB)' - (BA)' = B'A' - A'B' = BA - AB = - (AB - BA), Hence AB - BA is skew symmetric.
solution: We are Given: A' = A, B' = B (AB - BA) ' = (AB)' - (BA)' = B'A' - A'B' = BA - AB = - (AB - BA), Hence AB - BA is skew symmetric.
solution_html:
We are Given: A' = A, B' = B
(AB - BA)' = (AB)' - (BA)'
= B'A' - A'B'
= BA - AB
= - (AB - BA),
Hence AB - BA is skew symmetric.
