question_html: Find the matrix X so that {tex}X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
question_text: Find the matrix X so that {tex}X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \;\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {a + 4b} \\ {c + 4d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2a + 5b} \\ {2c + 5d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {3a + 6b} \\ {3c + 6d} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} On solving a + 4b = -7 and 2a + 5b = -8 & c + 4d = 2 and 2c + 5d = 4 we get a = 1, b = -2, c = 2, d = 0 {tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 2} \\ 2&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \;\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {a + 4b} \\ {c + 4d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2a + 5b} \\ {2c + 5d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {3a + 6b} \\ {3c + 6d} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} On solving a + 4b = -7 and 2a + 5b = -8 & c + 4d = 2 and 2c + 5d = 4 we get a = 1, b = -2, c = 2, d = 0 {tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 2} \\ 2&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \;\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} a&b \\ c&d \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 5 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {a + 4b} \\ {c + 4d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2a + 5b} \\ {2c + 5d} \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {3a + 6b} \\ {3c + 6d} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 7} \\ 2 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8} \\ 4 \end{array}\;\;\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 9} \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
On solving a + 4b = -7 and  2a + 5b = -8 & c + 4d = 2 and 2c + 5d = 4
we get a = 1, b = -2, c = 2, d = 0
{tex}X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 2} \\ 2&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
There are two families A and B. There are 4 men, 6 women and 2 children in family A and 2 men, 2 women and 4 children in family B. The recommended daily allowance for calories is Man: 2400, woman: 1900, child: 1800 and for proteins is Man: 55 gm, woman: 45 gm and child: 33 gm.
Represent the above information by matrices. Using matrix multiplication, calculate the total requirement of calories and proteins for each of the two families.
question_text: There are two families A and B. There are 4 men, 6 women and 2 children in family A and 2 men, 2 women and 4 children in family B. The recommended daily allowance for calories is Man: 2400, woman: 1900, child: 1800 and for proteins is Man: 55 gm, woman: 45 gm and child: 33 gm. Represent the above information by matrices. Using matrix multiplication, calculate the total requirement of calories and proteins for each of the two families.
explanation: The members of the two families can be represented by a 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 matrix F given below. F = {tex}\begin{array}{l}\\ {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\begin{array}{l} ~{M ~~ W~~~ C} \\ {\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]} \end{array}{/tex} and the recommended daily allowance of calories and proteins for each member can be represented by 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix R as given below. R = {tex}\mathop {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} M \\ W \\ C \end{array}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2400} \\ {1900} \\ {1800} \end{array}} \right.\left. {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {55} \\ {45} \\ {33} \end{array}} \right]}\limits^{{\text{Calories}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\text{Proteins}}} {/tex} The total requirement of calories and proteins for each of the two families is given by the matrix multiplication FR as given below. FR = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2400} & {55} \\ {1900} & {45} \\ {1800} & {33} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l} {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} {24600} & {556} \\ {15800} & {332} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, family A requires 24600 calories and 556 gm proteins and family B requires 15,800 calories and 332 gm proteins.
solution: The members of the two families can be represented by a 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 matrix F given below. F = {tex}\begin{array}{l}\\ {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\begin{array}{l} ~{M ~~ W~~~ C} \\ {\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]} \end{array}{/tex} and the recommended daily allowance of calories and proteins for each member can be represented by 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix R as given below. R = {tex}\mathop {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} M \\ W \\ C \end{array}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2400} \\ {1900} \\ {1800} \end{array}} \right.\left. {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {55} \\ {45} \\ {33} \end{array}} \right]}\limits^{{\text{Calories}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\text{Proteins}}} {/tex} The total requirement of calories and proteins for each of the two families is given by the matrix multiplication FR as given below. FR = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2400} & {55} \\ {1900} & {45} \\ {1800} & {33} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l} {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} {24600} & {556} \\ {15800} & {332} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, family A requires 24600 calories and 556 gm proteins and family B requires 15,800 calories and 332 gm proteins.
solution_html:
The members of the two families can be represented by a 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 matrix F given below.
F = {tex}\begin{array}{l}\\ {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex}{tex}\begin{array}{l} ~{M ~~ W~~~ C} \\ {\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]} \end{array}{/tex}
and the recommended daily allowance of calories and proteins for each member can be represented by 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix R as given below.
R ={tex}\mathop {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} M \\ W \\ C \end{array}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2400} \\ {1900} \\ {1800} \end{array}} \right.\left. {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {55} \\ {45} \\ {33} \end{array}} \right]}\limits^{{\text{Calories}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\text{Proteins}}} {/tex}
The total requirement of calories and proteins for each of the two families is given by the matrix multiplication FR as given below.
FR = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {4} & {6} & {2} \\ {2} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2400} & {55} \\ {1900} & {45} \\ {1800} & {33} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l} {A} \\ {B} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} {24600} & {556} \\ {15800} & {332} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, family A requires 24600 calories and 556 gm proteins and family B requires 15,800 calories and 332 gm proteins.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
A trust caring for handicapped children gets  ₹ 30,000 every month from its donors. The trust spends half of the funds received for medical and educational care of the children and for that it charges 2% of the spent amount from them, and deposits the balance amount in a private bank to get the money multiplied so that in future the trust goes on functioning regularly. What percent of interest should the trust get from the bank to get a total of ₹ 1,800 every month?
Use matrix method, to find the rate of interest. Do you think people should donate to such trusts?
question_text: A trust caring for handicapped children gets ₹ 30,000 every month from its donors. The trust spends half of the funds received for medical and educational care of the children and for that it charges 2% of the spent amount from them, and deposits the balance amount in a private bank to get the money multiplied so that in future the trust goes on functioning regularly. What percent of interest should the trust get from the bank to get a total of ₹ 1,800 every month? Use matrix method, to find the rate of interest. Do you think people should donate to such trusts?
explanation: Out of 30,000 rupees received, the trust spends half for medical and education care. Hence, the amount deposited in private bank is the remaining half = 15,000 rupees. The trust charges 2% of the money spent for medical and educational care, Hence the earning of the trust from the above charges is 2% of 15,000 = 15,000 * 2/100 = 300 rupees. Let the earning of the trust from the above charges be 'x' and the earning from interest obtained from the bank be 'y' We have the following system of equations: x + y = 1800; x = 300 The above two equations can be written in matrix form as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\frac{\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 300 \\ 1500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, we get Y = 1500. Let the monthly interest rate be R% Hence R% of 15,000 = 1500 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{R}{100} \times{/tex} 15,000 = 1500 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 10% Hence the rate of interest per month is 10%
solution: Out of 30,000 rupees received, the trust spends half for medical and education care. Hence, the amount deposited in private bank is the remaining half = 15,000 rupees. The trust charges 2% of the money spent for medical and educational care, Hence the earning of the trust from the above charges is 2% of 15,000 = 15,000 * 2/100 = 300 rupees. Let the earning of the trust from the above charges be 'x' and the earning from interest obtained from the bank be 'y' We have the following system of equations: x + y = 1800; x = 300 The above two equations can be written in matrix form as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\frac{\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 300 \\ 1500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, we get Y = 1500. Let the monthly interest rate be R% Hence R% of 15,000 = 1500 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{R}{100} \times{/tex} 15,000 = 1500 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 10% Hence the rate of interest per month is 10%
solution_html:
Out of 30,000 rupees received, the trust spends half for medical and education care.
Hence, the amount deposited in private bank is the remaining half = 15,000 rupees.
The trust charges 2% of the money spent for medical and educational care,
Hence the earning of the trust from the above charges is 2% of 15,000 = 15,000 * 2/100 = 300 rupees.
Let the earning of the trust from the above charges be 'x' and the earning from interest obtained from the bank be 'y'
We have the following system of equations:
x + y = 1800;
x = 300
The above two equations can be written in matrix form as
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\frac{\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ \gamma \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1800 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 300 \\ 1500 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, we get Y = 1500.
Let the monthly interest rate be R%
Hence R% of 15,000 = 1500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{R}{100} \times{/tex} 15,000 = 1500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 10%

Hence the rate of interest per month is  10%

question_html:
Three schools X, Y and Z organized a fete (mela) for collecting funds for flood victims in which they sold hand-held fans, mats and toys made from recycled material, the sale price of each being ₹25, ₹100 and ₹50 respectively. The following table shows the number of articles of each type sold:

School / Article X Y z
Hand-held fans 30 40 35
Mats 12 15 20
Toys 70 55 75

Using matrices, find the funds collected by each school by selling the above articles and the total funds collected. Also write anyone value generated by the above situation.
question_text: Three schools X, Y and Z organized a fete (mela) for collecting funds for flood victims in which they sold hand-held fans, mats and toys made from recycled material, the sale price of each being ₹25, ₹100 and ₹50 respectively. The following table shows the number of articles of each type sold: School / Article X Y z Hand-held fans 30 40 35 Mats 12 15 20 Toys 70 55 75 Using matrices, find the funds collected by each school by selling the above articles and the total funds collected. Also write anyone value generated by the above situation.
explanation: Let the funds collected by schools X, Y and Z be x, y and z (in ₹) respectively. So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}Fans & Mats & Toys \\30 & 12 & 70 \\ 40 & 15 & 55 \\ 35 & 20 & 75\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}cost\\25 \\ 100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 5450 \\ 5250 \\ 6625 \end{array}\right]{/tex} By equality of matrices, we get: x = 5450, y = 5250 and z = 6625 So the funds collected by the school X is ₹5450, by the school Y is ₹5250 and by the school is ₹6625 And, total funds collected =₹5450 + ₹5250 + ₹6625 =₹17325
solution: Let the funds collected by schools X, Y and Z be x, y and z (in ₹) respectively. So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}Fans & Mats & Toys \\30 & 12 & 70 \\ 40 & 15 & 55 \\ 35 & 20 & 75\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}cost\\25 \\ 100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 5450 \\ 5250 \\ 6625 \end{array}\right]{/tex} By equality of matrices, we get: x = 5450, y = 5250 and z = 6625 So the funds collected by the school X is ₹5450, by the school Y is ₹5250 and by the school is ₹6625 And, total funds collected =₹5450 + ₹5250 + ₹6625 =₹17325
solution_html:
Let the funds collected by schools X, Y and Z be x, y and z (in ₹) respectively.
So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}={tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}Fans & Mats & Toys \\30 & 12 & 70 \\ 40 & 15 & 55 \\ 35 & 20 & 75\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}cost\\25 \\ 100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 5450 \\ 5250 \\ 6625 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
By equality of matrices, we get: x = 5450, y = 5250 and z = 6625
So the funds collected by the school X is ₹5450, by the school Y is ₹5250 and by the school is ₹6625
And, total funds collected =₹5450 + ₹5250 + ₹6625 =₹17325

question_html: To raise money for an orphanage, students of three schools A, B and C organised an exhibition in their locality, where they sold paper bags, scrap-books and pastel sheets made by them using recycled paper, at the rate of Rs. 20, Rs. 15 and Rs. 5 per unit respectively. School A sold 25 paper bags, 12 scrap-books and 34 pastel sheets. School B sold 22 paper bags, 15 scrap-books and 28 pastel sheets while School C sold 26 paper bags, 18 scrap-books and 36 pastel sheets. Using matrices, find the total amount raised by each school. By such exhibition, which values are generated in the students?
question_text: To raise money for an orphanage, students of three schools A, B and C organised an exhibition in their locality, where they sold paper bags, scrap-books and pastel sheets made by them using recycled paper, at the rate of Rs. 20, Rs. 15 and Rs. 5 per unit respectively. School A sold 25 paper bags, 12 scrap-books and 34 pastel sheets. School B sold 22 paper bags, 15 scrap-books and 28 pastel sheets while School C sold 26 paper bags, 18 scrap-books and 36 pastel sheets. Using matrices, find the total amount raised by each school. By such exhibition, which values are generated in the students?
explanation: School Article A B C Paper Bags 25 22 26 Scrapbooks 12 15 18 Pastel Sheet 34 28 36 The number of articles sold by each school can be written in the matrix form as follows: {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The rate of each article can be written in the matrix form as follows : Y=[20 155] The amount collected by each school is given by {tex}\mathrm{YX}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 20 & 155 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex} YX=[850 805 970] Thus, the amount collected by schools A, B and C are Rs 850, Rs 805 and Rs 970, respectively. Total amount collected = Rs(850 + 805 + 970) = Rs 2,625 The situation highlights the helpful nature of the students.
solution: School Article A B C Paper Bags 25 22 26 Scrapbooks 12 15 18 Pastel Sheet 34 28 36 The number of articles sold by each school can be written in the matrix form as follows: {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The rate of each article can be written in the matrix form as follows : Y=[20 155] The amount collected by each school is given by {tex}\mathrm{YX}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 20 & 155 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex} YX=[850 805 970] Thus, the amount collected by schools A, B and C are Rs 850, Rs 805 and Rs 970, respectively. Total amount collected = Rs(850 + 805 + 970) = Rs 2,625 The situation highlights the helpful nature of the students.
solution_html:
School
Article A B C
Paper Bags  25 22 26
Scrapbooks 12 15 18
Pastel Sheet 34 28 36

The number of articles sold by each school can be written in the matrix form as follows:
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
The rate of each article can be written in the matrix form as follows :
Y=[20 155]
The amount collected by each school is given by 
{tex}\mathrm{YX}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 20 & 155 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} 25 & 22 & 26 \\ 12 & 15 & 18 \\ 34 & 28 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
YX=[850 805 970]
Thus, the amount collected by schools A, B and C are Rs 850, Rs 805 and Rs 970, respectively.
Total amount collected = Rs(850 + 805 + 970)
= Rs 2,625
The situation highlights the helpful nature of the students.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 5 } & { x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}, then find (x - y).
question_text: If {tex}2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 5 } & { x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , then find (x - y).
explanation: According to the question, {tex}2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 5 } & { x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 8 } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 8 + y } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x + 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}8 + y = 0{/tex} and {tex}2x + 1 = 5{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8 and {tex}x = \frac { 5 - 1 } { 2 } = 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y = 2 - (-8) = 10
solution: According to the question, {tex}2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 5 } & { x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 8 } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 8 + y } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x + 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}8 + y = 0{/tex} and {tex}2x + 1 = 5{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8 and {tex}x = \frac { 5 - 1 } { 2 } = 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y = 2 - (-8) = 10
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 5 } & { x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 8 } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { y } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 8 + y } \\ { 10 } & { 2 x + 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 7 } & { 0 } \\ { 10 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}8 + y = 0{/tex} and {tex}2x + 1 = 5{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8 and {tex}x = \frac { 5 - 1 } { 2 } = 2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y = 2 - (-8) = 10

question_html: If [x 1] {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= O, find x.
question_text: If [x 1] {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 1 & 0 \\ -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = O, find x.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = 0{/tex} Using matrix multiplication, {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\Rightarrow x - 2 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
solution: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = 0{/tex} Using matrix multiplication, {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\Rightarrow x - 2 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = 0{/tex}
Using matrix multiplication, 
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - 2 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}\Rightarrow x - 2 = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2

question_html: If A is a square matrix such that A2 = A. then write the value of 7A - (I + A)3. where I is an identity matrix.
question_text: If A is a square matrix such that A 2 = A. then write the value of 7A - (I + A) 3 . where I is an identity matrix.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}A^2 = A{/tex} Now, {tex}7A - (I + A)^3 = 7A - [I^3 + A^3 + 3I (I + A)]{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} (x + y) 3 = x 3 + y 3 + 3xy (x + y] = {tex} 7A - [I + A^2 A + 3A (I + A)]{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} I 3 = I ] =7A - (I + A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A + 3AI + 3A 2 ) [ {tex}\because{/tex} A 2 = A] = 7A - (I + A + 3A + 3A) [ {tex}\because{/tex} AI = A and A 2 = A] = 7A - (I + 7A) = -I
solution: According to the question, {tex}A^2 = A{/tex} Now, {tex}7A - (I + A)^3 = 7A - [I^3 + A^3 + 3I (I + A)]{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} (x + y) 3 = x 3 + y 3 + 3xy (x + y] = {tex} 7A - [I + A^2 A + 3A (I + A)]{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} I 3 = I ] =7A - (I + A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A + 3AI + 3A 2 ) [ {tex}\because{/tex} A 2 = A] = 7A - (I + A + 3A + 3A) [ {tex}\because{/tex} AI = A and A 2 = A] = 7A - (I + 7A) = -I
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}A^2 = A{/tex}
Now, {tex}7A - (I + A)^3 = 7A - [I^3 + A^3 + 3I (I + A)]{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} (x + y)3 = x3 + y3 + 3xy (x + y]
={tex} 7A - [I + A^2  A + 3A (I + A)]{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} I3 = I ]
=7A - (I + A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A + 3AI + 3A2) [{tex}\because{/tex} A2 = A]
= 7A - (I + A + 3A + 3A) [{tex}\because{/tex} AI = A and A2 = A] 
= 7A - (I + 7A) = -I

question_html: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - y } & { z } \\ { 2 x - y } & { w } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { 0 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}, then find the value of x + y.
question_text: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - y } & { z } \\ { 2 x - y } & { w } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { 0 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , then find the value of x + y.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - y } & { z } \\ { 2 x - y } & { w } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { 0 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\Rightarrow x - y = -1{/tex} ...(i) and 2x - y = 0 ...(ii) From equation (i) and (ii) x = 1 and y = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = 1 + 2 = 3
solution: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - y } & { z } \\ { 2 x - y } & { w } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { 0 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\Rightarrow x - y = -1{/tex} ...(i) and 2x - y = 0 ...(ii) From equation (i) and (ii) x = 1 and y = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = 1 + 2 = 3
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x - y } & { z } \\ { 2 x - y } & { w } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { 0 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}\Rightarrow x - y = -1{/tex} ...(i)
and 2x - y = 0 ...(ii)

From equation (i) and (ii)
x = 1 and y = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = 1 + 2 = 3

explanation: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x .y } & { 4 } \\ { z + 6 } & { x + y } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 8 } & { w } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\therefore \quad x \cdot y = 8{/tex} ...(i) z + 6 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = -6 ...(ii) and x + y = 6 ....(iii) On adding Eqs. solution: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x .y } & { 4 } \\ { z + 6 } & { x + y } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 8 } & { w } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}\therefore \quad x \cdot y = 8{/tex} ...(i) z + 6 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = -6 ...(ii) and x + y = 6 ....(iii) On adding Eqs. According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { x .y } & { 4 } \\ { z + 6 } & { x + y } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 8 } & { w } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}

question_html: If{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a+4 & 3 b \\ 8 & -6 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 a+2 & b+2 \\ 8 & a-8 b \end{array}\right]{/tex}, write the value of a - 2b.
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a+4 & 3 b \\ 8 & -6 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 a+2 & b+2 \\ 8 & a-8 b \end{array}\right]{/tex} , write the value of a - 2b.
explanation: According to the question,We are given that, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { 3 b } \\ { 8 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { b + 2 } \\ { 8 } & { a - 8 b } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}{/tex} {tex}{/tex} {tex}3b = b + 2 {/tex} ......(i) {tex}-6 = a - 8b{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}{/tex} On solving the Eqs. (i) and (ii) , we get, a = 2 and b = 1 . Now, a - 2b = 2 - 2(1) = 2 - 2 = 0
solution: According to the question,We are given that, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { 3 b } \\ { 8 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { b + 2 } \\ { 8 } & { a - 8 b } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}{/tex} {tex}{/tex} {tex}3b = b + 2 {/tex} ......(i) {tex}-6 = a - 8b{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}{/tex} On solving the Eqs. (i) and (ii) , we get, a = 2 and b = 1 . Now, a - 2b = 2 - 2(1) = 2 - 2 = 0
solution_html:
According to the question,We are given that, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { 3 b } \\ { 8 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 4 } & { b + 2 } \\ { 8 } & { a - 8 b } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}{/tex}{tex}{/tex}{tex}3b = b + 2 {/tex} ......(i)
{tex}-6 = a - 8b{/tex}...(ii) {tex}{/tex}
On solving the Eqs. (i)  and (ii) , we get,
a = 2 and b = 1 .
Now, a - 2b = 2 - 2(1) = 2 - 2 = 0

question_html: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x-y} & {z} \\ {2 x-y} & {\ w}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}{-1} & {4} \\ {0} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find x, y, z, w.
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x-y} & {z} \\ {2 x-y} & {\ w}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}{-1} & {4} \\ {0} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find x, y, z, w.
explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x-y} & {z} \\ {2 x-y} & {w}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{-1} & {4} \\ {0} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so x - y = -1 ....(i) z = 4 ....(ii) 2x - y = 0 ....(iii) w = 5 .....(iv) On solving equation (i) and (iii), we get x = 1 and y = 2 Hence, x = 1, y = 2, z = 4, w = 5
solution: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x-y} & {z} \\ {2 x-y} & {w}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{-1} & {4} \\ {0} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so x - y = -1 ....(i) z = 4 ....(ii) 2x - y = 0 ....(iii) w = 5 .....(iv) On solving equation (i) and (iii), we get x = 1 and y = 2 Hence, x = 1, y = 2, z = 4, w = 5
solution_html:
Given, 
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x-y} & {z} \\ {2 x-y} & {w}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{-1} & {4} \\ {0} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so
x - y = -1 ....(i)
z = 4 ....(ii)
2x - y = 0 ....(iii)
w = 5 .....(iv)
On solving equation (i) and (iii), we get x = 1 and y = 2
Hence, x = 1, y = 2, z = 4, w = 5

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If 2{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{3} & {4} \\ {5} & {x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find x and y.
question_text: If 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{3} & {4} \\ {5} & {x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find x and y.
explanation: Given, 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{3} & {4} \\ {5} & {x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6} & {8} \\ {10} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6+1} & {8+y} \\ {10+0} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{7} & {8+y} \\ {10} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so 8 + y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8 And 2x + 1 = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 5 - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 Hence, x = 2, y = -8
solution: Given, 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{3} & {4} \\ {5} & {x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6} & {8} \\ {10} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6+1} & {8+y} \\ {10+0} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{7} & {8+y} \\ {10} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so 8 + y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8 And 2x + 1 = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 5 - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 Hence, x = 2, y = -8
solution_html:
Given,
2{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{3} & {4} \\ {5} & {x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6} & {8} \\ {10} & {2 x}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{1} & {y} \\ {0} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{6+1} & {8+y} \\ {10+0} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{7} & {8+y} \\ {10} & {2 x+1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}{7} & {0} \\ {10} & {5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Since corresponding entries of equal matrices are equal, so
8 + y = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -8
And 
2x + 1 = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 5 - 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
Hence,
x = 2, y = -8

question_html: If matrix {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and A2 = kA, then write the value of k.
question_text: If matrix {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and A 2 = kA, then write the value of k.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}A^2 = kA{/tex} ...(ii) Now, A 2 = A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 + 1 } & { - 1 - 1 } \\ { - 1 - 1 } & { 1 + 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} [multiplying row by column] {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right] = 2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 = 2A [from Eq. (i)] Comparing with Eq. (ii), k = 2
solution: According to the question, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}A^2 = kA{/tex} ...(ii) Now, A 2 = A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 + 1 } & { - 1 - 1 } \\ { - 1 - 1 } & { 1 + 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} [multiplying row by column] {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right] = 2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 = 2A [from Eq. (i)] Comparing with Eq. (ii), k = 2
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}A^2 = kA{/tex} ...(ii)
Now, A2 = A{tex}\cdot{/tex}A
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 + 1 } & { - 1 - 1 } \\ { - 1 - 1 } & { 1 + 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} [multiplying row by column]
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right] = 2 \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A2 = 2A [from Eq. (i)]
Comparing with Eq. (ii), 
k = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html: For what value of x, is the matrix {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} a skew-symmetric matrix?
question_text: For what value of x, is the matrix {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} a skew-symmetric matrix?
explanation: If A is a skew-symmetric matrix, then A = -A T , where A T is transpose of matrix A According to the question, {tex} A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} A = -A T {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - x } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding element, {tex}\Rightarrow x = 2{/tex}
solution: If A is a skew-symmetric matrix, then A = -A T , where A T is transpose of matrix A According to the question, {tex} A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} A = -A T {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - x } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding element, {tex}\Rightarrow x = 2{/tex}
solution_html:
If A is a skew-symmetric matrix, then A = -AT, where AT is transpose of matrix A
According to the question,  {tex} A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
A = -AT
{tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { - 1 } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { x } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 0 } & { 1 } & { - x } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding element,
{tex}\Rightarrow x = 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find matrix A, if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find matrix A, if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: Given {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{9-1} & {-1-2} & {4+1} \\ {-2-0} & {1-4} & {3-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{8} & {-3} & {5} \\ {-2} & {-3} & {-6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: Given {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{9-1} & {-1-2} & {4+1} \\ {-2-0} & {1-4} & {3-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{8} & {-3} & {5} \\ {-2} & {-3} & {-6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex} + A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{9} & {-1} & {4} \\ {-2} & {1} & {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {0} & {4} & {9}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{9-1} & {-1-2} & {4+1} \\ {-2-0} & {1-4} & {3-9}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{8} & {-3} & {5} \\ {-2} & {-3} & {-6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_html: If{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a-b & 2 a+c \\ 2 a-b & 3 c+d \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find the value of b.
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a-b & 2 a+c \\ 2 a-b & 3 c+d \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find the value of b.
explanation: We are given that, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a-b & 2 a+c \\ 2 a-b & 3 c+d \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 \end{array}\right]{/tex} We need to find the value of b. We know by the property of matrices,for the equality of matrices, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} This implies, a 11 = b 11 , a 12 = b 12 , a 21 = b 21 and a 22 = b 22 Similarly,by equating the corresponding entries of the two matrices on the both sides of the matrix equation, we get the following equations. a – b = -1 …(i) 2a + c = 5 …(ii) 2a – b = 0 …(iii) 3c + d = 13 …(iv) We have the equations (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv). We need not solve equations (ii) and (iv). We will be able to solve for b from equations (i) and (iii). Multiply equation (i) by 2. a – b = -1 [× 2] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2a – 2b = -2 …(v) Subtracting equation (iii) from equation (v), 2a - 2b = -2 2a - b = 0 {tex}\mathop {\frac{{( - )( + )( - )}}{{0 - b = - 2}}}\limits_{\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -b = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 2 Thus, the value of b = 2.
solution: We are given that, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a-b & 2 a+c \\ 2 a-b & 3 c+d \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 \end{array}\right]{/tex} We need to find the value of b. We know by the property of matrices,for the equality of matrices, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} This implies, a 11 = b 11 , a 12 = b 12 , a 21 = b 21 and a 22 = b 22 Similarly,by equating the corresponding entries of the two matrices on the both sides of the matrix equation, we get the following equations. a – b = -1 …(i) 2a + c = 5 …(ii) 2a – b = 0 …(iii) 3c + d = 13 …(iv) We have the equations (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv). We need not solve equations (ii) and (iv). We will be able to solve for b from equations (i) and (iii). Multiply equation (i) by 2. a – b = -1 [× 2] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2a – 2b = -2 …(v) Subtracting equation (iii) from equation (v), 2a - 2b = -2 2a - b = 0 {tex}\mathop {\frac{{( - )( + )( - )}}{{0 - b = - 2}}}\limits_{\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -b = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 2 Thus, the value of b = 2.
solution_html:
We are given that,
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} a-b & 2 a+c \\ 2 a-b & 3 c+d \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
We need to find the value of b.
We know by the property of matrices,for the equality of matrices,
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
This implies,
a11 = b11, a12 = b12, a21 = b21 and a22 = b22
Similarly,by equating the corresponding entries of the two matrices on the both sides of the matrix equation, we get the following equations.
a – b = -1 …(i)
2a + c = 5 …(ii)
2a – b = 0 …(iii)
3c + d = 13 …(iv)
We have the equations (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv).
We need not solve equations (ii) and (iv). We will be able to solve for b from equations (i) and (iii).
Multiply equation (i) by 2.
a – b = -1 [× 2]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2a – 2b = -2 …(v)
Subtracting equation (iii) from equation (v),
2a - 2b = -2
2a - b = 0
{tex}\mathop {\frac{{( - )( + )( - )}}{{0 - b = - 2}}}\limits_{\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -b = -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 2
Thus, the value of b = 2.

question_html: If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}and A2 = pA, then write the value of p.
question_text: If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A 2 = pA, then write the value of p.
explanation: We are given a matrix A, as A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A 2 = pA We need to find the value of p. First, let us find A 2 . We know that, A 2 = A.A {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} In multiplication of matrices A and A, such that A 2 = Z(say): {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} z_{11} & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} For the calculation of z 11 : Dot multiply the 1 st row of first matrix and the 1 st column of second matrix and then sum up. (2 -2)(2 -2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 4 + 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 8 So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} For the calculation of z 12 : Dot multiply the 1 st row of first matrix and the 2 nd column of second matrix and then sum up. (2 -2)(-2 2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} -2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -4 – 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -8 So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ (-2 \times 2)+(2 \times-2) & (-2 \times-2)+(2 \times 2) \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -4-4 & 4+4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} …(i) Now, let us find pA. Multiply p by matrix A, pA = p {tex}\times\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} p \times 2 & p \times-2 \\ p \times-2 & p \times 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right] \end{array}{/tex} …(ii) Substituting value of A 2 and pA from equation(i) and equation (ii) in A 2 = pA, we have {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right]{/tex} We know that by the property of matrices,if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} This implies, a 11 = b 11 , a 12 = b 12 , a 21 = b 21 and a 22 = b 22 So, 2p = 8 -2p = -8 -2p = -8 2p = 8 Take equation, 2p = 8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex} ⇒ p = 4 Thus, the value of p = 4.
solution: We are given a matrix A, as A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A 2 = pA We need to find the value of p. First, let us find A 2 . We know that, A 2 = A.A {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} In multiplication of matrices A and A, such that A 2 = Z(say): {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} z_{11} & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} For the calculation of z 11 : Dot multiply the 1 st row of first matrix and the 1 st column of second matrix and then sum up. (2 -2)(2 -2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 4 + 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 8 So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} For the calculation of z 12 : Dot multiply the 1 st row of first matrix and the 2 nd column of second matrix and then sum up. (2 -2)(-2 2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} -2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -4 – 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -8 So, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ (-2 \times 2)+(2 \times-2) & (-2 \times-2)+(2 \times 2) \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -4-4 & 4+4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} …(i) Now, let us find pA. Multiply p by matrix A, pA = p {tex}\times\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} p \times 2 & p \times-2 \\ p \times-2 & p \times 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right] \end{array}{/tex} …(ii) Substituting value of A 2 and pA from equation(i) and equation (ii) in A 2 = pA, we have {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right]{/tex} We know that by the property of matrices,if {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex} This implies, a 11 = b 11 , a 12 = b 12 , a 21 = b 21 and a 22 = b 22 So, 2p = 8 -2p = -8 -2p = -8 2p = 8 Take equation, 2p = 8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex} ⇒ p = 4 Thus, the value of p = 4.
solution_html:
We are given a matrix A, as
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A2 = pA
We need to find the value of p.
First, let us find A2.
We know that, A2 = A.A
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}A^2{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
In multiplication of matrices A and A, such that A2 = Z(say):
{tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} z_{11} & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
For the calculation of z11: Dot multiply the 1st row of first matrix and the 1st column of second matrix and then sum up.
(2 -2)(2 -2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 4 + 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(2 -2) = 8
So,
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & z_{12} \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
For the calculation of z12: Dot multiply the 1st row of first matrix and the 2nd column of second matrix and then sum up.
(2 -2)(-2 2) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} -2 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -4 – 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 -2)(-2 2) = -8
So,
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ z_{21} & z_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Similarly,
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ (-2 \times 2)+(2 \times-2) & (-2 \times-2)+(2 \times 2) \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -4-4 & 4+4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
So,
{tex}A^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} …(i)
Now, let us find pA.
Multiply p by matrix A,
pA = p {tex}\times\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} p \times 2 & p \times-2 \\ p \times-2 & p \times 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} pA = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right] \end{array}{/tex} …(ii)
Substituting value of A2 and pA from equation(i) and equation (ii) in
A2 = pA, we have
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{cc} 8 & -8 \\ -8 & 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 p & -2 p \\ -2 p & 2 p \end{array}\right]{/tex}
We know that by the property of matrices,if
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} b_{11} & b_{12} \\ b_{21} & b_{22} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
This implies,
a11 = b11, a12 = b12, a21 = b21 and a22 = b22
So,
2p = 8
-2p = -8
-2p = -8
2p = 8
Take equation,
2p = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex}
⇒ p = 4
Thus, the value of p = 4.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 } & { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = A + \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { - 1 } \\ { 0 } & { 4 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}, then find the matrix A.
question_text: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 } & { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = A + \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { - 1 } \\ { 0 } & { 4 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , then find the matrix A.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 } & { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { - 1 } \\ { 0 } & { 4 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 - 1 } & { - 1 - 2 } & { 4 + 1 } \\ { - 2 - 0 } & { 1 - 4 } & { 3 - 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 8 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { - 2 } & { - 3 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Two matrices can be subtracted only when their orders are same.
solution: According to the question, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 } & { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { - 1 } \\ { 0 } & { 4 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 - 1 } & { - 1 - 2 } & { 4 + 1 } \\ { - 2 - 0 } & { 1 - 4 } & { 3 - 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 8 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { - 2 } & { - 3 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Two matrices can be subtracted only when their orders are same.
solution_html:
According to the question, 
{tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 } & { - 1 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { - 1 } \\ { 0 } & { 4 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 9 - 1 } & { - 1 - 2 } & { 4 + 1 } \\ { - 2 - 0 } & { 1 - 4 } & { 3 - 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 8 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { - 2 } & { - 3 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Two matrices can be subtracted only when their orders are same.

question_html:
Write the value of x - y + z from following equation.
{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {x + y + z} \\ {x + z} \\ {y + z} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{l}} 9 \\ 5 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
question_text: Write the value of x - y + z from following equation. {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {x + y + z} \\ {x + z} \\ {y + z} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{l}} 9 \\ 5 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
explanation: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { x + y + z } \\ { x + z } \\ { y + z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 9 } \\ { 5 } \\ { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}x + y + z = 9{/tex} ...(i) {tex}x + z = 5{/tex} ...(ii) and {tex}y + z = 7{/tex} ...(iii) Putting the value of x + z from Eq. (ii) in Eq. (i) {tex}y + 5 = 9{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y = 4{/tex} On putting y = 4 in Eq. (iii), we get z = 3 Again, putting z = 3 in Eq. (ii), we get x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y + z = 2 - 4 + 3 = 1
solution: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { x + y + z } \\ { x + z } \\ { y + z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 9 } \\ { 5 } \\ { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}x + y + z = 9{/tex} ...(i) {tex}x + z = 5{/tex} ...(ii) and {tex}y + z = 7{/tex} ...(iii) Putting the value of x + z from Eq. (ii) in Eq. (i) {tex}y + 5 = 9{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y = 4{/tex} On putting y = 4 in Eq. (iii), we get z = 3 Again, putting z = 3 in Eq. (ii), we get x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y + z = 2 - 4 + 3 = 1
solution_html:
According to the question, 
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { x + y + z } \\ { x + z } \\ { y + z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 9 } \\ { 5 } \\ { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}x + y + z = 9{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}x + z = 5{/tex} ...(ii)
and {tex}y + z = 7{/tex} ...(iii)
Putting the value of x + z from Eq. (ii) in Eq. (i) 
{tex}y + 5 = 9{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y = 4{/tex}
On putting y = 4 in Eq. (iii), we get z = 3
Again, putting z = 3 in Eq. (ii), we get x = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y + z = 2 - 4 + 3 = 1

question_html: Simplify {tex}\cos \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta } & { \cos \theta } \end{array} \right] + \sin \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin \theta } & { - \cos \theta } \\ { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex}.
question_text: Simplify {tex}\cos \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta } & { \cos \theta } \end{array} \right] + \sin \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin \theta } & { - \cos \theta } \\ { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} .
explanation: According to the question, {tex}\cos \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta } & { \cos \theta } \end{array} \right] + \sin \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin \theta } & { - \cos \theta } \\ { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta + \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&{ 1} \end{array}} \right]\quad \left[ {\because {{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta = 1} \right]{/tex} = I = unit matrix.
solution: According to the question, {tex}\cos \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta } & { \cos \theta } \end{array} \right] + \sin \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin \theta } & { - \cos \theta } \\ { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta + \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&{ 1} \end{array}} \right]\quad \left[ {\because {{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta = 1} \right]{/tex} = I = unit matrix.
solution_html:
According to the question,

 {tex}\cos \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta } & { \cos \theta } \end{array} \right] + \sin \theta \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin \theta } & { - \cos \theta } \\ { \cos \theta } & { \sin \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } & { \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta \cos \theta + \sin \theta \cos \theta } & { \cos ^ { 2 } \theta + \sin ^ { 2 } \theta } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&{ 1} \end{array}} \right]\quad \left[ {\because {{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta = 1} \right]{/tex}
= I = unit matrix.

question_html: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex}, write the value of x.
question_text: If {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , write the value of x.
explanation: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 - 6 } & { - 6 + 12 } \\ { 5 - 14 } & { - 15 + 28 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}x = 13{/tex} .
solution: According to the question, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 - 6 } & { - 6 + 12 } \\ { 5 - 14 } & { - 15 + 28 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Equating the corresponding elements, {tex}x = 13{/tex} .
solution_html:
According to the question,
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 2 - 6 } & { - 6 + 12 } \\ { 5 - 14 } & { - 15 + 28 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 4 } & { 6 } \\ { - 9 } & { x } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Equating the corresponding elements, 
{tex}x = 13{/tex}.

question_html: If {tex} 3 A - B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex} B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , then find the matrix A.
question_text: If {tex} 3 A - B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex} B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} , then find the matrix A.
explanation: According to the question, {tex} B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex} 3 A - B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 { A } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {{\text{put }}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 5 + 4 } & { 0 + 3 } \\ { 1 + 2 } & { 1 + 5 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 9 } & { 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 3 A = 3 \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
solution: According to the question, {tex} B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex} 3 A - B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 { A } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {{\text{put }}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 5 + 4 } & { 0 + 3 } \\ { 1 + 2 } & { 1 + 5 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 9 } & { 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 3 A = 3 \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex} B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex} 3 A - B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 { A } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + B = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {{\text{put }}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 5 + 4 } & { 0 + 3 } \\ { 1 + 2 } & { 1 + 5 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 9 } & { 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 3 A = 3 \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore A = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
